item.title: [BD-3965] adding more information on example for data consumption
item.description:
Request from Alan Zaki(alan.zaki@braze.com)

  • Request_Type
    • standard
  • Description
    • could we add additional information to the note maybe with an example: Updating a custom attribute object to null also consumes a data point.

ADD:
assuming I've got an array:
array_attribute: ["value1", "value2"]

array_attribute :

{ remove : [ "Value3" ]}

If I remove a value that does not exist, does it incur DP?
array_attribute :

{ remove : [ "Value1" ]}

If I remove a value that exist, does it incur DP?
Let's say if I run REST API - user/track request with custom_attribute_name : null information, is this request also consume the DP?
If com.appboy.enable_location_collection is set to true or yes, the profile data 'Most Recent Device Locale' will be automatically collected by Braze every time when users start session.
Then, will this automatic collection of 'Most Recent Device Locale' be counted toward datapoint consumption as follows or not?
Answer:

Any update to a Custom Attribute will be counted as a data point, including passing a null value, this includes if the value is already a null value.
In the case of an array a data point is consumed regardless of whether the element exists in the array or not as we are attributing the data point to our processing of the data.

Also for #3, 1 datapoint will be consumed for a session start and 1 datapoint for Most Recent Device Locale. So in that example it would be 2 in total.
When an empty value is passed (e.g., a custom attribute is created but has no associated value), it would still consume data points. Whenever Braze sets (or unset) a user's custom attribute value to anything, it consumes a data point.

Do note that datapoints will be consumed even if the payload has unchanging data as per our documentation

  • Request_Url
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/data_points?redirected=2#consumption-count
item.summary: adding more information on example for data consumption

comments.comment: KA - https://braze.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VP000000HImvYAG/view

Alan Zaki - Looks like your description is already included in the doc. 

Were you suggesting that we add more info? What would that be? Thank you!
Manjula Karunakaran I was referring to adding the examples and more information than the line added in the Note: section
Thanks Alan! Joshua McCrowell - I am not sure on what our guidelines are reg providing examples when do we provide vs when we do not. Would you have any guidance ? Thanks!

I don't know if there are guidelines per se–this is part of the work of curating the docs and making the articles up to date, relevant, searchable, findable, etc. 

I admit, I'm having some trouble parsing the KB article. I don't exactly understand the nuance here from the KB article itself. 

In its current state, I don't think the request to pull this exact text from the KB article into this docs page should go forward. The actual information that the KB article is trying to communicate needs to be pulled out and rearticulated. Just moving the KB article's examples into the documentation would harm the quality of the documentation, as it is not up to our standards.

item.title: [BD-3966] Email rendering information for Outlook
item.description:
Request from Alan Zaki(alan.zaki@braze.com)

  • Request_Type
    • standard
  • Description
    • I think we can add this information below in our client facing docs on email not rendering for outlook if this is a known issue:

An email campaign composed of a large image in the email body is failing to display said image when accessed in Outlook 2019/2016.

The image loads perfectly in the mobile Outlook App, Web App, iOS Mail, Gmail, and other email clients like Mailspring and Thunderbird.

Resolution:
In this case, the issue is isolated to the Outlook Desktop Client for Windows.
This email client is notorious for having issues displaying images and all workarounds revolve around having the end-user tweak their Outlook settings.

In this support thread on Microsofts website, you can see that many other users have reported this issue but at the moment Microsoft has not provided an update.

If there is no error message, but a warning instead: "If there are problems with how this message is displayed, click here to view it in a web browser.
Click here to download pictures. To help protect your privacy, Outlook prevented automatic download of some pictures in this message."

A potential workaround is to decrease the size of the image itself with an image resizing tool instead of using CSS or HTML.
This will require several tests to find the right size of the image. This workaround is not guaranteed to work on each inbox because there are a lot of settings a user can toggle on and off on the Desktop App that can prevent the email from looking as intended or displaying images at all.

Additionally, older versions of Outlook do not use a web engine to render HTML but a Microsoft Word engine which causes all sorts of unintended behavior. The only workaround for those versions (Prior 2016) is to write specific inline CSS to achieve the desired result.

  • Request_Url
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/help/help_articles/email/html_looks_strange
item.summary: Email rendering information for Outlook

comments.comment: KA - https://braze.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VP000000Cx17YAC/view
Joshua McCrowell - do we add special cases to our docs? Like issues isolated to Outlook Desktop for eg?

Manjula Karunakaran In general, the docs should be about the product's behavior. Troubleshooting is a part of that! But when the issue is on the client side, like with Outlook, I consider that troubleshooting an Outlook issue not a Braze issue. 

A link in that KB article goes to a Sendgrid page called "How to Keep Your Email Looking Great in Any Inbox." I can imagine us having an equivalent article, allowing for these sorts of troubleshooting tips. But feels like translating a single KB article into that sort of request is out of scope. 

I'd be hesitant to add this specific note without that sort of context into our docs, unless it would truly deflect a heavy Support burden and is a question that comes up very frequently. Is it?

item.title: [BD-3978] Adding content card testing for web in our docs
item.description:
Request from Alan Zaki(alan.zaki@braze.com)

  • Request_Type
    • standard
  • Description
    • While integrating the Web SDK, is it possible to test the content card integration?
      Yes. Using the browser developer tools (Chrome Console) you can test the content cards. Follow these steps:
      Create a content card campaign
      Target your test user.
      Log in to the website that has the Web SDK integration
      Open the browser console: Right click on the page > Inspect Page > Console
      Use the method:
      window.Appboy.getCachedContentCards
      appboy.display.toggleContentCards
  • Request_Url
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/web/content_cards/#prerequisites
item.summary: Adding content card testing for web in our docs

Joshua McCrowell - I am not very certain how to go about it. The content of this card would be suitable if we have a Troubleshooting section under content cards just like we have in Web IAM . 
Thank you!

item.title: [BD-3980] Add more details about what happens when an email campaign or Canvas is stopped.
item.description:
Request from Evelin Jaqueline da Silva(evelin.dasilva@braze.com)

  • Request_Type
    • standard
  • Description
    • Current Description:
      Users will be prevented from entering the Canvas and no further messages will be sent out. For email campaigns and Canvases, the stop button does not mean that send will immediately stop. This is because when the send requests are sent out, they cannot be stopped from being delivered to the user.

Improved description:
Users will be prevented from entering the Canvas and no further messages will be sent out. For email campaigns and Canvases, the stop button does not mean that send will immediately stop. This is because when the send requests are sent out, they cannot be stopped from being delivered to the user. After all, once a request is sent to the ESP (Sparkpost or Sendgrid) it will be processed and sent even if the campaign or canvas has been stopped.

While Braze will not send any further requests to ESP since the campaign/canvas is stopped, we might still see an increment in the campaign/canvas analytics based on the number of requests that ESP is still processing.

  • Request_Url
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/faq/#what-happens-when-an-email-campaign-or-canvas-is-stopped
item.summary: Add more details about what happens when an email campaign or Canvas is stopped.

comments.comment: Hey Evelin da Silva ! Thanks for this ticket! We've placed it in our backlog, and it will be worked according to our prioritization process. That said, we're currently tied up with high-priority items. If you would like to try making a community contribution, we have instructions on how you can update the docs here. If you do update the docs, I'd be more than happy to provide support, and am available to answer any questions you might have, review your update, and make sure its merged in promptly. Just let me know!
KA link for context - https://braze.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VP000000DvGbYAK/view
Joshua McCrowell as you may have guessed, this is part of the migration project. I have updated the Epic & the KA link as well. Thank you!
I have tagged this to Email team but not sure if the doc is owned by Email or CLX. Thank you!

item.title: [BD-3986] Adding FAQ on page below
item.description:
Request from Alan Zaki(alan.zaki@braze.com)

  • Request_Type
    • standard
  • Description
    • Do we think it could help to add an FAQ with the below question: Why is the Analytics found between the Canvas and Funnel Report different?

While analysing both Canvas step level analytics and it's Funnel report, a customer find that the results are different at the Canvas step level where the analytics show a higher figure than the Funnel report.

They have the same date range, so why the discrepancy?

The time range for the canvas step conversion analytics are just any conversions that happened in that timeframe. Depending on what conversion window they choose and how long the canvas is, the parameter could be larger than 14 days for conversions on the canvas step.

In the Analyze Variants page, the data that is presented on that specific tab represents the events/actions that happened during the timeframe that was selected. How many users entered the canvas on those dates, how many users performed the conversion events on those dates, and etc. If you select dates from 01 of Jan to 07 of Jan for example. Let's say I have user A that entered the canvas on the 01 of September and performed the conversion event on the 08. The canvas entry for that user would be reflected in those numbers (1 entry). However, the conversion event wouldn't, since it was performed after the 07 of Jan (0 event performed).

Now, for the funnel report, it works a bit differently. When you create (or edit the report), you also select a timeframe. However, that timeframe refers to "Users who entered the Canvas during those dates". So, if I select the exact same timeframe for the funnel report (from 01 of Jan to 07 of Jan), it would show different data. For the user A example, it would show both the canvas entry and also the conversion event (1 entry and 1 event performed). This happens because it is showing data for users that entered the canvas during the timeframe, and it is accounting their actions for the next 14 days after the entry (depending on how you setup the report).
The analytics a customer sees on the canvas itself represent all possible conversions / behaviours / engagement, whereas the funnel report puts restrictions on the order of events as well as the timing of the events.

Expected behaviour here is that these are not expected to match.

  • Request_Url
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/analytics/reporting/funnel_reports
item.summary: Adding FAQ on page below

comments.comment: KA - https://braze.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VP000000GepVYAS/view
Joshua McCrowell - I could not access the Slack thread in the KA myself but I feel this would need some confirmation from Reporting team as well . Is this something you could help us with? Thanks!

Manjula Karunakaran I think following up with the Product teams on a case by case basis for these is going to get out of scope. I do think it's something we should be doing in general, though. Let's follow up in our next meeting.
Joshua McCrowell Sounds good. Thank you!

item.title: [BD-4005] HTTP 400 Bad Request error thrown in a webhook campaign. What does it mean?
item.description:
1. Migration request: We are missing a good troubleshooting step in the docs.
  2. Knowledge gap: We have this in the KA: "If you're using Liquid to template personalization tags within your webhook request, make sure that they do not result in a blank value or contain any JSON-breaking characters."

This seems to be a great step to take in finding out why a webhook may be returning 400.

  1. Recommended actions: If we could amend the 400 error docs to under steps to resolve that would be great.
  2. Explanations for actions (optional): The KA is almost identical to our docs except this, but I think checking the liquid is really important here.
  3. Flag sensitive information: Just in the Internal Notes fiels.
  4. Related PQ:

  5. *Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/help/help_articles/api/webhook_connected_content_errors/#4xx-errors*

item.title: [BD-4009] Funnel Reports FAQ
item.description:
1. Migration request: Can we have a FAQ for funnel reports in our docs? I believe the docs cover most of the questions, but this format is easier for customers to digest as the article was created based on the type of question the technical support team has been receiving about the topic.
  2. Knowledge gap: The article covers some best-practices aligned with use cases from customers, such as:

Is there a limit to the number of events that can be included in a funnel report?
 
While there is no explicitly defined limit, 4-6 events should cover most use cases based on best practices. Reports with an excessive number of events may lead to delays or potential time outs.

  1. Recommended actions: I would create a section for FAQ as we have for email here: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/faq
  2. Explanations for actions (optional): We have been populating this article with questions customer answer when creating a case about funnel reports.
  3. Flag sensitive information: The image within the article doesn't need to be migrated. We also have this confluence page answering some questions, but not sure if this is up to date: https://confluence.braze.com/display/REP/Funnel+Report+FAQs
  4. Related PQ: N/A
  5. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/analytics/reporting/funnel_reports/#setting-up-funnel-reports
item.summary: Funnel Reports FAQ

item.title: [BD-4010] Click Tracking Only Available for Links Directed to a HTTP or HTTPS Site (Can you click track phone numbers? etc.)
item.description:
1. Migration request: This information must be shared on the track click linking page so customers are aware of this.
  2. Knowledge gap: The article highlights something that is a requirement and this is not available in our docs.
  3. Recommended actions: We should add:
    "Click Tracking Only Available for Links Directed to a HTTP or HTTPS Site. If you do not add "HTTP(S)" before the URL, the link would not get branded. ** 
    Share examples of the email as a malito (that won't be tracked)
    Malito:
    href="mailto:your email address
    Instead of a mailto, you can use a url link that would direct them to your website: href="https://yourwebsiteinformation/contact-us""
    "
    This warning is also important: Edits made to an existing template won’t be reflected in campaigns that were created using previous versions of that template.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): Customers reach out asking why some links are not tracked.
  5. Flag sensitive information: N/A
  6. Related PQ: N/A
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/analytics/tracking/email_tracking
item.summary: Click Tracking Only Available for Links Directed to a HTTP or HTTPS Site (Can you click track phone numbers? etc.)

item.title: [BD-4015] Best practices on handling how emails show in dark mode vs light mode
item.description:
1. Migration request: Provide tips and suggestions on how we can approach setting up emails and some workarounds or best practices for handling dark mode vs light mode email rendering.
  2. Knowledge gap: As dark mode is a functionality / preference controlled on the device and by inbox providers such as Gmail and Outlook where they invert the colours of your messages, it's not something that can be easily controlled from the Braze side. However we have come across some workarounds if you are using the HTML editor.
  3. Recommended actions:  Workarounds and Best Practices are provided in the KA and we can include these.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional):  Dark mode might not show up as expected in inboxes like Gmail and the customer will ask us why and how to fix it.
  5. Flag sensitive information: If there is any information in the Salesforce article that should not be migrated and should remain internal, please highlight it specifically so that we do not accidentally make that information public.
  6. Related PQ:  NA
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/drag_and_drop/faq#can-i-preview-how-my-email-appears-in-dark-mode
item.summary: Best practices on handling how emails show in dark mode vs light mode

comments.comment: We have some really good best practices for the dark mode in the KA. With about 34 cases linked to this topic, it is worthwhile to share some of these best practices with our customers . However , I would recommend that we discuss this with product as well, regarding the extent to which we suggest best practices.

item.title: [BD-4023] CSV Export User Data vs. CSV Export Email Addresses
item.description:
Please can you add the detail from this article to the Export FAQs

https://braze.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VP000000IiIjYAK/view

Specifically: 
Issue
What's the difference between CSV Export User Data vs. CSV Export Email Addresses on the analytics page for a campaign/canvas?
Cause
There's some differences when exporting user data either when selecting between User Data and Email addresses.
Resolution
Clicking the CSV Export Email Addresses link (as opposed to the CSV Export User Data link) will only download data for users with email addresses. So if you have a segment of 100K users, but only 50K of those users have an email address, and you click the CSV Export Email Addresses link, then you should only see 50K rows in the CSV.CSV Export User Data will export all recipient data.
item.summary: CSV Export User Data vs. CSV Export Email Addresses

comments.comment: Emma Dewdney please also remember to link the SF Knowledge Epic. Thank you!
Rachel - We don't have a section for Exporting Segments in our documentation. May be good t o include this difference here.  https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/segments

item.title: [BD-4026] I am getting an Unsubscribe Link warning despite having an unsubscribe link how can I resolve this?
item.description:
1. Migration request: If users already have an unsubscribe link added, but they still see the warning telling them to add one, they can ignore the warning, as long as they have confirmed they do have an unsubscribe link added.
  2. Knowledge gap: Docs don't tell users why this warning pops up even though they already added an unsubscribe link or what to do about it.
  3. Recommended actions: We can tell customers that if they have already confirmed that the unsubscribe link has been added, and they still see this warning, they can safely disregard the warning. There are also workarounds to remove the warnings: Method 1 - For HTML emails, select "Plaintext Editor" and click on "Regenerate from HTML". Method 2 - Alternatively, after duplicating the campaign, duplicate the Variant, and then immediately remove the original variant. (Note: Do not click on the original variant, as it will cause the warning to appear on the new variant too. Only click on the "X" of the original variant to remove it.)
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): This warning will persist if an affected email campaign is duplicated.
  5. Flag sensitive information: This may be a bug as the internal notes say "Bug confirmations:
    https://brazetechnology.slack.com/archives/C02V2J5G0EN/p1678723561345669
    https://brazetechnology.slack.com/archives/C02V2J5G0EN/p1685429671218289"
  6. Related PQ: no pq linked
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/faq#can-braze-track-unsubscribe-links-counted-towards-the-unsubscribe-metric
item.summary: I am getting an Unsubscribe Link warning despite having an unsubscribe link how can I resolve this?

comments.comment: Suggested to include specifically Method 1 as a "workaround" . We have 25 cases attached to this card , for which this solution seems to have worked.

If a customer is not seeing any results for the email opt-in/opt-out data query. Double check on the customer dashboard that they have an email subscription group. This query is specifically for email subscription groups."

Stephany Jane Aragon - checking in here please - Wdyt about my above suggestion? Thank you!
Hi Manjula Karunakaran yeah, I agree that it is too specific for the public facing docs. we can take that out of the suggestions to add

item.title: [BD-4048] Deep Links on Android Not Working
item.description:
1. Migration request: This KA is a troubleshooting guide for clients to go through prior to reaching out to support. It would be great to have this guide in our public-facing docs. ** It might also be nice to have a section at the end of this guide that says "Still experiencing issues? Reach out to support here" with a link to create a case with support. 
  1. Knowledge gap: Our docs do contain information about deep links for Android however on that page there is no (To my knowledge) troubleshooting guide for when things do not function as they should.
  2. Recommended actions: We should add the following steps for clients to work through issues with their Deeplinks involving android devices. This way when they come to support we can find the issue faster:

From the KA: 
Before escalating your issues to the Braze Support team, there are a couple of things you could check to troubleshoot the deeplink issue you're encountering on Android. We recommend working with your Android developers in going through these steps:

    • Check if the deeplink works when opened on other apps. If you open the deeplink using Slack, Notes or etc, does it open your app and redirect to the correct section of your app? If it does not, it is likely that your deeplink is not configured correctly. Kindly work with your developers in implementing/creating deeplinks with your app following this Android documentation. 
       
    • If the issue is that clicking the push notification with a deeplink does not open the app, check if you have enabled Braze to automatically open your app and any deep links when a push notification is clicked.
      • Documentation
      • File to check: braze.xml
      • Code to find/check: 
<bool name="com_braze_handle_push_deep_links_automatically">true</bool>
      • Please note that this can also be set via runtime configuration.
      • If this is not set, you should set it to true to enable Braze to automatically open your app and any deep links when a push notification is clicked.
         
    • If the issue is that the on-click behavior of the message does not result in the deeplink to redirect correctly. Here are things to look out for:
      1. If the link is sent via multiple channels (such as in-app message and push) does it work in either?
      2. Confirm whether you have implemented the IBrazeDeeplinkHandler. In most cases, the delegate is set in the app's Application.onCreate(). 
        • Documentation
        • File to check: Main Application class
        • Code to find: 
BrazeDeeplinkHandler.setBrazeDeeplinkHandler()
        • If a custom BrazeDeeplinkHandler has been implemented, kindly check if you are handling the links correctly. You can add breakpoints in your code to debug where in your implementation could be causing problems. You can also refer to the default IBrazeDeeplinkHander of the Braze Android SDK. 
      1. Check the verbose logs for messages that include the deep link URL contained in the message. 
        • File to check: verbose logs and AndroidManifest.xml
        • Logs to find: Could not find appropriate activity to open for deep link <deeplink URL>
        • If you see this in the verbose logs, this indicates that the deeplink URL is not registered. 
Check the AndroidManifest.xml and verify the Deep Link Configuration. If the deeplink is example://test, you should see the <data android:scheme="example" android:host="test" /> in the AndroidManifest.xml: 
<activity android:name=".SampleActivity">
    <intent-filter>
        <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
        <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
        <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
        <data android:scheme="example" android:host="test" />
    </intent-filter>
</activity>

If you cannot find an intent filter that contains the deeplink, kindly work with your developers in implementing/creating deeplinks with your app following this Android documentation. 
 

    • Lastly, you can test your deep links following this Android documentation.

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): Add it to the following Docs page (Towards the bottom) Or possibly create a Troubleshooting dedicated page and link it to this page: https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/android/push_notifications/deep_linking/#prerequisites

Note: There's a paragraph mentioning Verbose logs, it might be good to Hyper link over to the Verbose/Braze Logging docs we have.
item.summary: Deep Links on Android Not Working

comments.comment: May be good to get SDK team to take a look and see how much detail can we go thru in our public docs. have a list of pre-reqs to submit to support would be great. May be SDK debugger can address some of this and we could indicate that in the troubleshooting section?

item.title: [BD-4052] Currents: "You do not have any remaining Currents integrations"
item.description:

  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs say that "Currents are included with certain Braze packages. Contact your Braze representative if you have any questions or want to gain access." and that might be sufficient, but if users see "You do not have any remaining Currents integrations", they might not know why or what to do.

Or that the integration might be in a different workspace.

item.title: [BD-4061] Clicking on links within emails redirects to the “Your connection is not private.” error page.
item.description:
1. Migration request: This KA goes over troubleshooting a "Your connection is not private." error that occurs when a user clicks on a link within an email. This is one of those knowledge sources that we give clients after they send in a ticket to support.
  2. Knowledge gap: If we provide these steps within our public Knowledge base clients can go ahead and run the Dig command test to see if that's the issue. This way we can avoid an unnecessary support ticket being created.
  3. Recommended actions: To the public docs you need to add possibly a red warning that mentions if they see the error "Your connection is not private." when clicking their links is most likely due to a configuration error.

Then provide the Dig command steps to run the test and recommend they go through the steps once more to verify they're correct.

From the KA: 
The error - "Your connection is not private" indicates that your SSL or CDN is not configured correctly. ** 

You can run a dig command for your tracking domain in the Terminal to confirm where it points to.

    • Mac Search Bar > Search Terminal > Open Terminal
    • Run the following: dig cname (Tracking Domain)
    • Look at the Answer Section.
      If it is pointing to your ESP(Sendgrid/sendgrid.net or Sparkpost/spgo.io), then this is caused by misconfiguration.

In order for our SSL Click Tracking to work, this should point to your CDN. That said, kindly coordinate this with your team managing your SSL and CDN configuration. You can refer to our documentation here for the step-by-step guides written by Sendgrid and Sparkpost on how to configure your CDN. 

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):

This could probably go here: *https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/email_setup/ssl/#acquiring-an-ssl-certificate*
item.summary: Clicking on links within emails redirects to the “Your connection is not private.” error page.

comments.comment: May just need the key phrase "Your connection is not private" to be associated with cdn set up, so that customers know they should check that aspect before contacting Braze support

item.title: [BD-4074] What are IP warming best practices?

From the KA:

Before starting IP Warming, there are a few steps to take:

  1. Go into Manage App Group > Email Settings and do the following:
    1. Set the default domain for outgoing email in Outbound Email Settings(ensure you use the {!domain/domains} that we have set up for you- {!enter your domains here})
    2. _Recommended _Set up your Custom Footer, ensuring that you include a valid unsubscribe link
    3. Optional Custom Unsubscribe Page, Custom Opt In Page, Resubscribe Users When Their Email Changes
    4. _Recommended _Turn on List-Unsubscribe Header
  2. Set up Frequency Capping for Email in Global Campaign Settings
  3. Upload any required email templates into Templates and Media

item.title: [BD-4075] ChangeUser() SDK method FAQ
item.description:
1. Migration request: Explains to customers the expected behaviour when using the SDK method ChangeUser()
  2. Knowledge gap: We do not have a concise place explaining about these behaviour and warning customers about what might happen if they do not use it right.
  3. Recommended actions: Add in our public docs, in the begining of their SDK integration, the details below.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): 
    A few notes on how changeUser() method functions:
    • Calling changeUser() multiple times with the same user ID does not affect session count
    • changeUser() can also be called when an anonymous user creates a profile to assign them a user ID
      • If this user ID already exists, the anonymous user's data will not be merged into the identified profile
      • If calling changeUser() with a new user ID, the anonymous user's data will be merged into the new identified profile and a new session begins immediately
    • When you request a user switch (which is any call to changeUser where the new user ID is not the same as the existing user ID), the current session for the previous user (anonymous or not) is automatically ended and a new session is started.
    • You should not call changeUser() when the user logs out
    • You should not call changeUser() to a default profile, as this will prevent anonymous users and log all data to one external id, potentially causing other features to not behave as expected
  5. Flag sensitive information: N/A
  6. Related PQ: N/A
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): I'm not sure the best doc to add these information, because this is about all SDKs (iOS, Android and Web).
item.summary: ChangeUser() SDK method FAQ

item.title: [BD-4076] Sending To Multiple Profiles With the Same Email Address
item.description:
1. Migration request: Most of the content is available here and here, but it should be all in a single place as this is confusing for customers. 
  2. Knowledge gap: We should also provide more examples to illustrate how Braze works.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    I believe we should add everything about duplicate email here: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/best_practices/duplicate_emails/#email-subscription-updates including the question:
    https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/faq/#what-happens-when-an-email-is-sent-out-and-multiple-profiles-have-the-same-email-address
    Also, we should let customers know that we don't prevent them from creating duplicated email addresses. Something like:
    User profiles and e-mails don't have a 1:1 relationship in Braze, and e-mail addresses are not unique. We don't enforce unique email addresses for users, but we recommend that clients monitor for unique emails internally when new users are created.

Then add a real example of how the dedup works:

Same dispatch ID
For example, if two users (external_id 1 & 2) share the same e-mail address. When sending campaigns/canvas steps to both users in the target audience, Braze de-duplicates and enqueues 1 message per email address if it's in the same dispatch. This is regardless of how many user profiles share a given e-mail address ( i.e can be more than 2 ). An example where it will be in the dispatch is if you target external_id 1 & 2 in a scheduled send immediately e-mail campaign.

Note that users are deduplicated before any Liquid logic runs. One user will be selected to get the email, and the others will be deduplicated, even if the selected user's message aborts while templating the email. This scenario would result in none of the users receiving the email. 

Differing dispatch ID
However, we do not de-dupe messages if they are sent in differing dispatches. Therefore, it is possible for 1 email address (shared by two different users) to receive a campaign with no re-eligibility 2 different times. It is also worth noting that re-eligibility for email campaigns/steps is based on user data, not email. This can cause multiple emails to arrive in a user's inbox prior to the conclusion of the ineligibility timespan. Example of a differing dispatch ID, scheduled send to external_id 1 first, and then sending to external_id 2 5 minutes later.

Seed Group Members sharing an email with a Non Seed Group user
If a campaign is sent to an email address that is shared with multiple users, the message may be sent to a user that is not in the seed group but has the email address specified.

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): N/A
  2. Flag sensitive information: N/A
  3. Related PQ: N/A
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/best_practices/duplicate_emails/#message-sending-behavior

I noticed that Emma Dewdney  created this https://braze.atlassian.net/browse/BD-3930. The article that she mentioned is covering additional information that should also be availble on the doc https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/best_practices/duplicate_emails/#message-sending-behavior{}
item.summary: Sending To Multiple Profiles With the Same Email Address

item.title: [BD-4077] When Does Conversion Tracking Begin?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Explain when Conversion Tracking Begin canvases and campaigns
  2. Knowledge gap: We are not answering this question in our docs.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    For Campaigns:

Conversion tracking begins when a user receives the campaign, or they enter the campaign's control group.

Receiving a campaign and entering a campaign variant typically happen at the same time for campaigns. For in-app messages, tracking begins when a user logs an impression.

For Canvases:
**

For canvases, conversions are tracked when the user enters the canvas.

For Canvas steps, conversions are tracked for the duration they are in the Canvas step. If the user moves onto another step, the conversion tracking stops for the first step and starts for the second step. 

 

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): Customers ask this question.
  2. Flag sensitive information: N/A
  3. Related PQ: N/A
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 
    Campaign: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/campaigns/building_campaigns/conversion_events/#primary-conversion-event

*Canvas: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/canvas/faqs/#how-are-user-conversions-tracked-in-a-canvas*
item.summary: When Does Conversion Tracking Begin?

item.title: [BD-4078] What is the minimum time interval between triggers for IAMs?
item.description:
1. Migration request: There are some recomendations that are not available in our public docs.
  2. Knowledge gap: This information is missing and can improve their SDK integration.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    In production apps, we do not recommend making this value any smaller than 10, to avoid spamming the user with notifications. In our sample apps, we have reduced that time to 5 seconds.

Are there any downsides to setting the minimal time interval between triggers to 0?
You can reduce the interval below 5 seconds, but our suggestion is just to test at 0 seconds because if a user should receive both IAMs and they have not yet dismissed IAM 1, then IAM 2 isn't expected to trigger. The console logs would show Cannot show in-app message test because another message is being shown.That's the reasoning why the default is 30 seconds.

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): N/A
  2. Flag sensitive information: N/A
  3. Related PQ: N/A
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 
    https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/android/in_app_messages/delivery?redirected=2#minimum-time-interval-between-triggers
    https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/web/in_app_messages/delivery?redirected=2#minimum-time-interval-between-triggers
    https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/swift/in_app_messages/message_delivery/#delivery-semantics
item.summary: What is the minimum time interval between triggers for IAMs?

item.title: [BD-4079] Segment Users Who Clicked Link Alias
item.description:
1. Migration request: Our docs on Link Tracking segmentation are good, but I think there are a few nuances this KA points out that can be added to really flush out our Public Docs. 
  2. Knowledge gap: The KA info (added below) has a few nuances written out, like how to remove the tracking and what will happen if they take the link aliasing off and create a segment. We should add the missing information to our Public docs to flush it out further. Also add screenshots to our Public docs just like the KA uses here. It helps for visual learners.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    From the KA:
    It is possible to add a segment rule to identify users who have clicked a tracked link (link alias). This is only available where the link alias has tracking enabled (the checkbox on the left side in the image below).

    Tracking Links

The tracked link alias is available to use in a new segment filter, under the rule for 'clicked link alias'. For the link alias to show in the segment filter, link tracking must be enabled as above.
If the campaign is archived, link tracking will be disabled and the link alias cannot be used in a new filter. Existing filters using this link alias will still show, but new clicks may not be logged in some circumstances.

If link tracking is enabled after the campaign has been launched, only subsequent sends will include the tracked link. Any messages sent before the link tracking was enabled will not retroactively include the link alias.

If a link under link management ever had tracking enabled and was clicked on in a send, the campaign will show as an available option to select in the drop down for the segment filter even if link tracking has since been disabled. 

If the link is being tracked in Canvas draft mode, you will not be able to select the tracked link in the drop down menu. You'll need to ensure that the track link is updated to the Active/Live Canvas in order to be able to select the link in the drop down menu of the filter 'Clicked Alias in Canvas Step'.
 

Removing Tracked Links

If you have links that are out of scope but not untracked they will still populate in as an available link for segment filtering. 


To set links as untracked, navigate to Manage Settings > Email Settings > Link Aliasing Settings in the dashboard.

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): Add this to the Segment Filters paragraph for these docs: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/templates/link_aliasing/#action-based-filters
item.summary: Segment Users Who Clicked Link Alias

item.title: [BD-4080] Formatting for DateTime datatype?

Keep in mind that if not setting a timezone, or setting timezone incorrectly, we will always default to UTC. Time will always appear in company timezone on the dashboard. So if you wanted to set the time for October 31, 2019 6pm EST it would look as follows:

2019-10-31T18:00:00+04:00

If data sent in the wrong format the date will display an incorrect value.

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): N/A

item.title: [BD-4081] Firebase SenderID Mismatch
item.description:
1. Migration request: Covers how to troubleshoot a "SENDER_ID_MISMATCH" error regarding the Android Firebase
    2. Knowledge gap: We have an explanation of the error in our Docs but not how to troubleshoot/resolve it.
    3. Recommended actions: We get a lot of cases where clients are reporting this issue and need help resolving it. We should just provide the client this info off the bat to help them be more self-sufficient. 
    4. Explanations for actions (optional): 
      From KA:
      To fix this:
       
      1. To find the proper Firebase Server Key to replace it: Go to the Firebase console for your app
      2. Next to “Project Overview”, click on “Project Settings”
      3. In the “Cloud Messaging” tab, check to see that the SenderID below the API keys matches the one in Braze located beneath the Cloud Messaging API key.
      4. Warning: Do not change your SenderID in your Braze dashboard. Doing so will cause existing push registrations to be invalidated. If the SenderID does not match, then you need to find your Firebase project with the matching SenderID.
      5. Copy the “Server Key” under “Project credentials”. For more details please see Google’s Documentation.
      6. Input this server key into the “Cloud Messaging API Key” section of the App Settings page where you noted the corresponding outdated key(s).
      7. Click “Save”.
      8. To verify everything is working correctly, send a test push to a device before and after you change the API key without opening the application. This will ensure that users are continuing to receive push notifications without the need for a new push registration_id (push token) needing to be generated.
    5. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): Add this to the "Error:MissmatchSenderID" section of these docs: https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/android/push_notifications/troubleshooting/#push-notifications-bounced
item.summary: Firebase SenderID Mismatch

item.title: [BD-4082] Configuring Segment and Braze Destination Settings
item.description:
1. Migration request: Warm customers about possible integration issues.
  2. Knowledge gap: There are some best-practices available in our docs, but some are missing and we should add those.
  3. Recommended actions: Missing best-practices:
    Wrong API key inputted: 'App Identifier' vs `REST API Key'
    'App Identifier' = App API Key found in the "Manage App Group" or "Developer Console" page on the Braze Dashboard. This field is necessary for SDK integrations to work.
    REST API Key` = Dashboard Rest API Key for making API calls. Make sure the key has permission to access the users/track endpoint.

Which 'Braze Data Center' to input
Braze Data Center = Cluster. By setting the 'Braze Data Center', Segment will pull in the appropriate rest endpoint (e.g. https://rest.iad-01.braze.com) in order to make server-to-server calls.

Confusion on why the Segment event tester is not working when all settings look correct
Segment's event tester uses our API endpoint and will throw a 401 "Invalid API Key" if the correct 'Custom REST API Endpoint' is added in the settings.

Switching to a different source does not appear to work immediately.
Segment keeps the configuration settings in the cache for a long time, so when configuring a new source (i.e. switching from Cloud to Device Mode) the app may not show anything new until the cache renews.

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): These issues were found when troubleshooting cases related to segment.io
  2. Flag sensitive information: Please, do not add what is under the Internal Notes section.
  3. Related PQ: N/A
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 
    *https://www.braze.com/docs/partners/data_and_infrastructure_agility/customer_data_platform/segment/segment/#best-practices*
item.summary: Configuring Segment and Braze Destination Settings

item.title: [BD-4083] When do Action Based Delivery Campaigns Evaluate the Segment - Before or After the Trigger Event Occurs?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Explain to customers when Action Based Delivery Campaigns Evaluate the Segment.
  2. Knowledge gap: This information is not explicitly in our docs.
  3. Recommended actions: Add a question and an answer:
    When do Action Based Delivery Campaigns Evaluate the Segment - Before or After the Trigger Event Occurs?
    Segment membership is evaluated before the trigger event has occurred. You may also configure campaigns to re-evaluate just before send time.  This can be useful for instances where there is a delay between the trigger event and the actual send where the user could log the trigger event, then fall out of the audience before the send time.
  1. Explanations for actions (optional): It was created to explain the behaviour.
  2. Flag sensitive information: Internal notes section cannot be added because of the confluence page.
  3. Related PQ: N/A
  4. *Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/campaigns/building_campaigns/delivery_types/triggered_delivery/#why-did-a-user-not-receive-my-triggered-campaign*

item.description:

  2. Knowledge gap: Most of the information in the knowledge article is in our docs but it doesn't go over when a user is evaluated in a campaign with rate limit.  
  3. Recommended actions: recommend adding the internal notes section to our docs. 
    For campaigns with rate limit:

If this is an action campaign and the option to re-evaluate is set, then yes it will be re-evaluated before send.  If this is a scheduled campaign, the criteria will always be evaluated.

The criteria re-evaluation would apply for any kind of delay involved in sending, whether it be rate limiting, local time, intelligent time, or set in the trigger schedule, etc.

item.title: [BD-4097] Unsubscribe Link Not Working for Test Sends
item.description:
1. Migration request: To send yourself a test email, there are a few additional steps that are currently not listed in our documentation.
  2. Knowledge gap: If the client needs to send themselves a test email using an email address not associated with any user on the dashboard, the unsubscribe link will not work. Below are the steps that need to be added to resolve an error that will occur.
    Recommended actions: 
    From the KA: 
    When performing a Test Send, the Unsubscribe link will not work if the Email Address used for the test send is not a user on the App Group/Workspace.

In order to test that functionality, you may want to consider:

1) Using the email address of a test user that is already in the app group/workspace, or
2) Add your email address as a test user on the app group/workspace.

Alternatively, you may also preview the message as a test user on the app group/workspace, by searching for the user within the preview and test page:

And select "Override recipients' attributes with current preview user's attributes":

The unsubscribe URL for that test user will be sent with the test message to the test recipients.

Note: If you are facing an error on Google Chrome, such as, "insecure connection", this error might be attached to your cache browser and not necessarily with the Unsubscribe page.
 

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/sending_test_messages/#sending-a-test-push-notification-or-in-app-messages-
item.summary: Unsubscribe Link Not Working for Test Sends

comments.comment: May be worth including in our docs in some format - given that we have a few tickets of end users getting confused about this. Thanks

item.title: [BD-4119] Unsubscribes via a custom unsubscribe link metric
item.description:
Please add the detail from the KB article into this Braze doc:

'There's currently no way for Braze to identify that the clicks via a custom unsubscribe link are unsubscribed actions. The only way to accomplish this is for you to use an API call to update any user profiles where you record unsubscribes from your custom link.

So, if you host your own unsubscribe link and input that into an email, then these clicks are not automatically counted towards the 'Unsubscribe' metric.

The metrics within the Subscription Group Time Series will still show data for the unsubscribes as this is based on API calls.'

 

https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/reporting_and_analytics/analytics_glossary#unsubscribers-or-unsub
item.summary: Unsubscribes via a custom unsubscribe link metric

Emma Dewdney could you add the KB link to this case please?

Also, please do not miss to add the SF Epic and the number of linked cases. These fields are important for the tracking of the project. Thanks so much!
https://braze.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VP000000IPhVYAW/view

item.title: [BD-4129] Does Braze Support a Per Second Rate Limit?
item.description:
Migration request : Add notes to advise that Braze does not support 'per second' rate limiting for message sending.

Knowledge gap : Currently per second rate limiting is not mentioned.

Recommended Actions :  Add the explanation such as 

"We do not support a per second rate limit.

The way that our "X messages per minute" rate limit code works is that we randomly schedule up to X messages to go out in the course of a minute.

However, the code is not adaptive, meaning that it does not adjust itself based on how long it takes the endpoint to respond. So let's say that you're sending 600 per minute and are sending 1,200 messages in total. It will take roughly 2 minutes. For minute 2, our code will have scheduled 600 messages to go out.

However, it is possible that if the connected content API calls are slow to respond, that not all of the messages from minute 1 will have finished by the time the second minute starts. This can lead to what I call a "pile on effect" where we can end up with more than 600 API calls being made in a minute, or more than 600 concurrent connections to the API.

Even if we offered a per second rate limit, we would have the same problem unless we constantly adjusted the rate limit to take into consideration how long it took the API to respond. That's what we mean by adaptive, in that instead of releasing 600 per minute, our code knew how many we wanted to send, how long it took to send the most recent ones, and then dynamically adjusted how many more connected content calls to attempt to keep firmly within the set limit. "

Docs Page : https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/campaigns/building_campaigns/rate-limiting/#delivery-speed-rate-limiting
item.summary: Does Braze Support a Per Second Rate Limit?

comments.comment:
Paul Gladwell would like your opinion here

"Note that rate-limited messages may not be sent evenly over the course of each minute. Using the example of a 10,000-per-minute rate limit, this means Braze makes sure no more than 10,000 messages are sent per minute. This could mean a higher percentage of the 10,000 messages are sent within the first half minute versus the last half minute."

We have the above blurb in this page. 
Do you think the above statement can be refined - instead of stating that we do not support a per second rate limit? Reason I ask is, I believe we try to stay away from "we do not"  as much as possible in our Docs. Thank you!

item.title: [BD-4164] Campaigns Targeting New Users Surfaced Race Conditions
item.description:
1. Migration request: Provide extra details on how race conditions can occur per messaging channel. 
  2. Knowledge gap: 
    1. Our docs don't mention how race conditions (when targeting new users) can be encountered, depending on the messaging channel that the campaign/canvas uses. For example, lots of clients want to present an IAM during or right after their onboarding process, which will often result in the IAM not displaying to the user at the expected time. 
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. With In-App messages, the race condition situation can be a little more complicated.  The IAM has to be loaded on the device before being triggered.  If the trigger event is part of the onboarding process, or the user falls out of the segmentation for the custom event as part of their first session,  it's likely the user will not see the IAM.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): 
    1. Considering that sending messaging during user onboarding for their app is a common practice/use case of our clients, it would be great to give them awareness to these potential race conditions for app-related channels (IAM and content cards), and how the client could overcome that with changes to their data sending practices or campaign/canvas setup. 
  5. Flag sensitive information: N/a
  6. Related PQ: N/a
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 
    1. https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/testing/race_conditions/#types-of-race-conditions
item.summary: Campaigns Targeting New Users Surfaced Race Conditions

item.title: [BD-4203] What is the Difference Between a Full Sync and a Partial Sync for Content Cards?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Provide more information about full sync vs partial sync for content card refresh.
  2. Knowledge gap: Braze docs does not currently have this information, although it is available in this article: https://www.braze.com/resources/articles/9-tips-and-tricks-for-braze-content-cards#:~:text=%236%3A%20Get%20Familiar%20with%20Your%20Syncing%20Options 
  3. Recommended actions: Under the suggested docs page, the following can be added as a paragraph: 
    "Here are two syncing options available within Braze when you’re working with Content Cards:
    • Full Sync: Each time the changeUser() method is called (or every 7 days), Braze will perform a Full Sync by default and this pulls all of the Content Cards a given user is eligible for from our server.
    • Partial Sync: In comparison, Partial Syncs pull down only the new Content Cards the user is eligible for from our server. If a user isn't eligible for any new Content Cards, the request will return 0 cards. Partial syncs occur when requestContentCardsRefresh() is called.
      • Partial syncs limit the burden on our servers and the device's battery life. Content Cards once received are stored on the SDK locally so while Partial Syncs can return 0 Content Cards, the user will still see the available Content Cards they are eligible for."
  1. Explanations for actions (optional): N.A
  2. Flag sensitive information: Do not include any info under "Internal Notes"
  3. Related PQ: N.A.
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/content_cards/customizing_cards/feed/#refreshing-the-feed **
item.summary: What is the Difference Between a Full Sync and a Partial Sync for Content Cards?

comments.comment:
Interestingly, we have some mention of it https://www.braze.com/resources/articles/9-tips-and-tricks-for-braze-content-cards

but yes, would be good to have it in the content card doc itself.

item.title: [BD-4209] Link aliasing for URLs generated via Liquid (append with ? or &)
item.description:
1. Migration Request:
    • Improve documentation related to the Drag and Drop (DnD) Editor and Content Blocks in emails to ensure clarity on link handling and Liquid syntax.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • Current documentation does not adequately explain the necessity of including '?' before '#' in URLs within the DnD Editor and the implications of Liquid syntax on link management in Content Blocks.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Drag and Drop Editor:
        • When using a link in DnD with a button, the link must include a '?' before the '#' sign.
        • Example:
          {{https://www.braze1.com/to/LiIuQi52?#user_id=$ {user_id}

          &firstname=$

          {first_name}

          &ug_followup_segment=custom_attribute.$

          {UG_followup_segment}

          }}

        • Explanation: The link will not appear as a link alias in 'Link Management' because anything after the '#' sign is ignored, and the link does not make it back to us. Since there is Liquid in the URL, the system does not know how to automatically add '?' or '&'. The parameter must be inserted before the '#' sign, requiring manual addition of the question mark.
      • Content Block in Email:
        • When a link in the content block (e.g., https://braze.com/\{{offer_id{}}}) lacks the proper markup, it isn't picked up by link management.
        • Explanation: The system doesn't recognize this because there is Liquid in the URL without any '?' or '&', preventing safe appending. To fix this, simply append a '?' or '&' to the link in the content block and save the Content Block.
        • The system maps one link in the body to a single link ID. For analytics based on which offer ID was clicked, ingesting the current click event with the full URLs is recommended.
      • Using a Conditional Liquid Statement:
        • For displaying one of multiple links, it is better to assign the URL in an HTML block and then template the value.
        • Example Implementation:

liquid
Copy
{{{% if {{custom_attribute.$

{last_pro_app_active}

}} != blank or {{custom_attribute.$

{signup_source_product}

}} contains 'Pro' or {{custom_attribute.$

{last_spot_trade_transaction_ts}

}} != blank %}

{% assign url = 'https://krakenpro.onl/OBT6/verify' %} {% else %} {% assign url = 'https://kraken.onl/UflE/account' %} {% endif %}

}}

        • You can then template the URL into the URL field of the button like:
          {{url?}}
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing clear guidelines and examples on this page, we can reduce user errors related to link formatting, improve the effectiveness of link management, and enhance overall user experience with Braze's features: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/templates/link_aliasing/#link-aliasing-in-content-blocks
item.summary: Link aliasing for URLs generated via Liquid (append with ? or &)

item.title: [BD-4210] Images Not Included in Heatmap Export (CORS Error)
item.description:
1. Migration Request:
  • Add a note or troubleshooting section to the Braze public documentation regarding issues with images not being included in heatmap exports due to CORS errors.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
  • The current documentation does not address potential issues users may encounter when exporting heatmaps, specifically related to broken images and CORS policy errors.
  1. Recommended Actions:
  • Update the documentation to include:
  • Note: Images Not Included in Heatmap Export (CORS Error)
  • Summary: Images may appear broken when exporting the heatmap or may not be exported at all.
  • Issue:
  • When clicking the export heatmap button, users may see broken images within the exported file, accompanied by the error message:"One or more images failed to export. Try to configure your images to allow CORS or contact Braze Support."
  • In the Google Chrome developer console, users may encounter:ImageURLLine has been blocked by CORS policy: The 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header has a value 'CustomerDomain' that is not equal to the supplied origin. Have the server send the header with a valid value, or, if an opaque response serves your needs, set the request's mode to 'no-cors' to fetch the resource with CORS disabled.
  •  
  • Cause: This error occurs when CORS is not enabled for the specific image URLs, which may lead to access being denied by default.
  • Resolution:
  • Enable CORS for the image URLs to ensure they render correctly in the export.
  • Ensure that image assets support cross-origin sharing by having the server return the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header with either a wildcard * or the Braze dashboard domain.
  • Note that this setting is outside Braze's control, requiring collaboration with the development team.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
  • By including this information, we can help users troubleshoot issues related to heatmap exports, improving their experience and understanding of the functionality. This proactive approach can reduce support inquiries and enhance user satisfaction.

5.Flag sensitive information: n/a

6. Related PQ: n/a

7. *Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/reporting_and_analytics/email_reporting/#email-performance*
item.summary: Images Not Included in Heatmap Export (CORS Error)

item.title: [BD-4211] Message Extras and Currents
item.description:
1. Migration Request:
  • Enhance the Braze public documentation regarding the message_extras Liquid tag to include additional details about its usage, configuration, and limitations.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
  • The current documentation provides a basic overview of the message_extras Liquid tag but lacks comprehensive information on its application across different channels, potential issues with syntax, and the implications of using Liquid variables.
  1. Recommended Actions:
  • Update the documentation to include:
  • Definition:
  • "Message Extras is a catch-all term for key-value pair data that can be included with the message. The configuration of these key-value pairs depends on the channel. For example, in emails, these are added to the header, but they are added to the payload in iOS push."
  • Documentation Reference:
  • "Our documentation on setting up key-value pairs (Message Extras) is [here]."
  • Data Availability:
  • "Message Extra data is included in the message send events sent via Currents. Note that this data is only available in the send/impression tables (i.e., users.Messages.Email.Send)."
  • Syntax Warning:
  • "Be mindful of the message extra syntax; a malformed syntax would mean the extras would not be sent to Currents."
  • Example:Liquid
    Copy {% message_extras :key test :value 123 %}
  • Nesting Limitation:
  • "You cannot nest additional Liquid tags within message_extras. However, you can include Liquid variables as a key or value."
  • Example:Liquid
    Copy {% assign value = '123' %}
  • {% assign key = 'test' %}
  • {% message_extras :key key :value value %}
  • Webhook Support Confirmation:
  • "Confirmation from Product that webhook is supported for the message_extras Liquid tag, and Amplitude should be able to see it."  (Mentioned in this slack thread link: Slack thread link)

 

  • Size Limit:
  • "There is a 980kB size limit for individual fields in Currents."
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
  • By providing this additional information, we can enhance user understanding of the message_extras Liquid tag, reduce potential errors in implementation, and improve the overall effectiveness of message data handling in Braze.

 

5.Flag sensitive information: **

Slack Thread Reference:

  • Slack thread link

6.Related PQ: n/a

7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/personalization_and_dynamic_content/liquid/advanced_filters/message_extras
item.summary: Message Extras and Currents

item.title: [BD-4224] What is the difference between Push Enabled and Background Push Enabled as a filter option?
item.description:
Description:
This ticket addresses the need to clarify the differences between "Push Enabled" and "Background Push Enabled" in Braze documentation. The goal is to ensure users understand the specific conditions and implications of each filter.

Migration request:
We would like to migrate content that explains the differences between "Push Enabled" and "Background Push Enabled." For example, “Clarify the conditions under which users are considered Push Enabled versus Background Push Enabled.”

Knowledge gap:
Currently, there may be confusion among users regarding how these two filters are utilized. Understanding the distinction is crucial for users to effectively manage their push notification strategies and ensure they are using the correct settings for their campaigns. This information is important as it can enhance user experience by preventing misconfigurations that could lead to ineffective push notifications.

Recommended actions:
Add a section that clearly outlines the differences between "Push Enabled" and "Background Push Enabled." This should include:

  • A detailed explanation of what each term means.
  • The specific conditions that must be met for each filter.
  • Examples of scenarios where each filter would apply, such as how background push functionality can be used for tracking uninstalls and enabling silent push notifications.

Flag sensitive information:
There is no sensitive information in the current content that needs to be flagged for internal use only.

Related PQ:
n/a

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
To add Push FAQ section here: https://www.braze.com/docs/help/faqs
item.summary: What is the difference between Push Enabled and Background Push Enabled as a filter option?

The support document refers to the segment filters, as you noticed, while the public-facing article explains the push enablement concept in general. So, we can consider that this information already exists (in a different context) in the public documentation.

item.title: [BD-4226] Why Do Emails Land in the Spam Folder?
item.description:
• Note - I selected "Email" as the related team, though this documentation request is more associated with the Email Deliverability Services team.
  1. Migration request: Explain to customers the most common reasons their marketing emails would land in the spam folder. Help them self-serve their deliverability concerns
  2. Knowledge gap:  We have email address collection guidelines and sending best practices included across a few different sections in our docs and blog posts. The existing resources might be helpful for a marketer during initial setup, but for seasoned Braze users who run into Deliverability problems (low opens, emails landing in Spam), there should be a one-stop-shop to self-serve. Why are our marketing emails landing in Spam? Why is our Open-rate so low?
  3. Recommended actions:
    1. Structure the "Email" section of the docs so that customers can get Deliverability resources in the fewest clicks possible. For example, the user could navigate to braze.com/docs -> click "Message Building by Channel" click Email -> click [new] "Improve Deliverability".
    2. Add a new page for "Improve Deliverability". This page should include the content from the "Why Do Emails Land in the Spam Folder?" Salesforce Article, and links out to some of the existing docs and blog posts created by our Deliverability Experts.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): There are a ton of reasons why a customer's emails could be landing in spam, or end-users aren't opening them. Having a one-stop-shop for this information will help educate our customers with the lowest lift / search time for them, and make them better email marketers overall.
  5. Flag sensitive information: Let's remove the line "Businesses that don't change and innovate, die. The same is true with email marketing--change or die.". I feel the "change or die" language fits in a blog post style communication, but it's too intense for our public docs.
  6. Related / Existing resources:
    1. Sunset policies
    2. IP Warming
    3. Deliverability pitfalls and spam traps
    4. Know before you send
    5. Consent and address collection
    6. Email FAQ
    7. Blog - Deliverability Indicators: Understanding Spam Complaints and What They Mean For Customer Engagement
    8. Blog - Destination Inbox: Navigating the Modern Landscape of Email Deliverability
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 
    • New page within the Email section called "Improve Deliverability"
    • Let me know if you'd like to screenshare to talk through it!
item.summary: Why Do Emails Land in the Spam Folder?

item.title: [BD-4233] Can you delete campaigns/canvases?
item.description:
Knowledge Gap : The canvas and campaign documentation, although covers archival, it does not specifically say that users cannot delete campaigns and canvas'.

 

Recommendations :

Add the following clarifications to the Canvas and Campaign archival notes :

"Campaigns/Canvases/Segments etc., can't be fully deleted, however they can be archived. 

Campaigns/Canvases that have been archived

  • will not show up in their respective overview pages by default
  • will not send messages
  • have their enabled flags set to false
  • will show up in filter drop-downs
  • can still continue to receive conversions"

Canvas : https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/canvas/managing_canvases/archiving_canvases

 

Campaign : https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/campaigns/managing_campaigns/archiving_campaigns

item.title: [BD-4234] How do I use the spacer.gif resource?
item.description:
Knowledge gap : the use of the spacer.gif in webhooks for a canvas step is not mentioned in our docs.

 

Recommended actions :

Add the following into the Canvas documentation for a webhook step :

"We have a hack of our webhooks by calling a resource we host called spacer.gif. The spacer.gif image will not be sent or displayed to any users, but the webhook call will be successfully sent. This acts as a "dummy" step in canvas, so everyone technically gets it but no one actually receives or sees the message.

  1. Select Webhook as your message channel
  1. Select Blank Template
  1. Enter in http://braze-images.com/spacer.gif in the Webhook URL field
  1. Go to Settings (gear icon in upper right), and select POST as HTTP Method.

This will actually do nothing, and enable you to move users straight on in the Canvas journey and separate the audience on the first (but actually it's the second) step."

Docs link : https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/webhooks/creating_a_webhook/?tab=canvas#step-1-choose-where-to-build-your-message
item.summary: How do I use the spacer.gif resource?
