item.title: [BD-4237] Why are Emails Not Rendering As Expected In Actual Sends
item.description:
1. Migration request: Provide users with more context around why HTML renders differently for different ESPs.
  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs mention some brief troubleshooting steps, but this KB article goes more in depth as to the causes of this issue and gives a bit more troubleshooting detail that our customers could find helpful.
  3. Recommended actions: Add section on causes of emails rendering differently in different inboxes, and a note that while Support can offer some guidance in these instances, we cannot usually debug or troubleshoot custom HTML rendering issues. The content of the current KB is as follows:

Cause
The short answer is that this can often be expected behaviour, though it is not desirable.
After Braze sends out the email, it reaches different Email providers (e.g. Gmail, Outlook), all of which can interpret the same HTML code differently.
This is a recognised issue in the email industry, which happens due to the existence of many players in the ecosystem.
How each email client interprets and renders the HTML code is beyond the control of Braze.

Resolution

Check for Changes to the HTML

We recommend first checking that the email is received as expected. Braze will make very few changes to HTML when it is sent. You can check the received HTML by downloading a copy of the email as a HTML file. 
The only significant change Braze makes to the HTML is that the CSS is inlined.
CSS inlining is recommended as some mail clients will not render CSS. Inlining is a process by which the styles are applied to the elements directly.
Consider the Structure and Design of the HTML
If the HTML is received as expected, then the behavior will be because of the HTML itself. As mentioned previously, each mail client may render identical HTML differently. If this is the case, then we advise that you code the email differently.
Please note, while we can usually offer some general purpose guidelines, we cannot usually debug or troubleshoot custom HTML rendering issues.

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page: https://www.braze.com/docs/help/help_articles/email/html_looks_strange/#email-rendering
item.summary: Why are Emails Not Rendering As Expected In Actual Sends

item.title: [BD-4238] Email Campaign Has More Unique Clicks than Unique Opens
item.description:
1. Migration request: Explain to users why unique clicks may be higher than unique opens in some instances.
  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs discuss the definitions of unique clicks and unique opens, and we have a page about bot clicks and one about the open tracking pixel, but we don't discuss how those things can interact to inflate unique click numbers.
  3. Recommended actions: Add a brief section explaining the cause of this issue and what to look for when trying to troubleshoot. The current KB article includes this information:

Cause
The number of unique clicks may be higher than the number of unique opens for one of the following reasons: * The open tracking pixel was not loaded by the recipient's mailbox application.

    • This can occur if the message is clipped. If the open tracking pixel is at the end of the message and the message is large, it will not be loaded.
    • If the message reaches the spam folder, remote images aren't loaded.
    • Some recipients mailboxes may be configured for higher security. This is often the case for many corporate customers. Images may not be loaded by default (unless a user specifically overrides this). 
  • Bot actions
    • This could be due to bot click activity, such as an email security software on the recipient's end. Here, an open won't be logged, but the link in the message will be automatically clicked by the software.
      Resolution
      It is possible to determine whether the open tracking pixel was included in the raw message content. If the open tracking pixel was included as expected, the discrepancy is likely due to one of the other reasons above. 

Unfortunately, it isn't always possible to confirm whether the clicks were performed by actual end users.

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/reporting_and_analytics/email_reporting/#email-performance (The "Email Metrics" subsection of this page)
item.summary: Email Campaign Has More Unique Clicks than Unique Opens

item.title: [BD-4239] User Received an Email Campaign Twice
item.description:
1. Migration request: Expand on information about email send deduplication and when it does/does not happen.
  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs cover some basic details about when we do and do not deduplicate email sends, but there is information in this KB article that is not included in our public-facing docs that, if included, could cut down on support tickets by allowing customers to self-serve their troubleshooting needs more easily.
  3. Recommended actions: This is the information in the KB article that could be useful to include in our public docs:

There are three scenarios that can cause a user to receive an email twice:

    • Usage error at campaign/canvas creation
      • In this case, users do not receive the email twice, but instead, receive two separate emails with the same subject line. Sometimes, when a campaign/canvas is duplicated, it is easy to miss some basic email configuration like images or the subject line of the email. A simple way to analyze whether this is the case or not is by looking closely at the user profile and analyzing each canvas and campaign that the user has received. It is important to check the change logs to see if the campaign/canvas was modified post launch since it is possible that the campaign/canvas had the same subject line as the original campaign when the user received it.
    • Multiple profiles with email forwarding enabled
      • If a user has multiple accounts in a given app but one of the accounts has email forwarding, the user will receive the campaign once to each inbox but appear twice in the inbox where the email is forwarded. Some email providers will let the user know if an incoming email is forwarded from another account, however, not all email providers do this.
    • Email configuration at the user end
      • There are a lot of email clients today that allow you to merge different email inboxes. This is very common on enterprise setups where the users email configuration is managed by an IT department. Some email clients call this feature Universal Inbox. Examples of these email clients that support Universal Inbox are: Outlook, HubSpot, Mailbird, ClientFlow, Mailspring, etc. In the cases where users have multiple accounts on a given app and the same campaign targets both, it looks in the inbox of the user as if they received a campaign twice in their inbox when in reality, the campaign simply sent to two users that happen to share the same inbox. 
      • To confirm if this is the case or not, the end user can confirm if they have multiple email accounts set as a universal inbox.
    • Campaign was Sent Twice
      • Particularly noticeable when the number of messages sent is significantly larger than the number of users in the audience. Can be confirmed via 'Audit Logs' -> 'Single Campaign Sent Log' in Internal Tools. This will tell you every time a campaign was launched and how many users it was sent to.
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/faq/#what-happens-when-an-email-is-sent-out-and-multiple-profiles-have-the-same-email-address
item.summary: User Received an Email Campaign Twice

item.title: [BD-4240] Why do my emails get clipped/truncated in Gmail and show a 'View Entire Message' option?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Give users some potential mitigation steps for preventing email clipping in Gmail.
  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs explain that emails can be clipped due to size, and give some content recommendations, but don't go into as much detail about what can be done to prevent clipping.
  3. Recommended actions: This is the KB article information that would be helpful to have in our docs:

To avoid clipping, the first recommendation is to edit the content and shorten it as much as possible. This includes copy, images, links, etc. It is also worth noting that it is best practice to have between 7 and 12 links in an email.

Once you have looked at the content and cut it down as much as possible, here are other steps to consider to trim down the size of the email:

    • Ensure that inline CSS is disabled - Within the Email Settings tabs in Braze, you can set this to be unticked automatically on all campaigns. (This will not work retrospectively)
    • Removal of all whitespace in the HTML
    • Compressing all creative assets and HTML within the email body.
    • Minify the HTML - There are online tools and plugins available that can achieve this, like the website below:
      • In the Braze Email campaign, uncheck in-line CSS
      • Copy the HTML in a Minifier website (for example [+https://www.willpeavy.com/tools/minifier/+]) then click "Minify"
      • Copy/Paste the minified version of the code in the Braze HTML editor
      • Send yourself a test and see the email displayed in full in your inbox
        • Please note that Minify will affect opens
    • Remove Plain Text Version - As links in plain version emails are also tracked, another way to minimize the size is to remove that plain text version. We would advise doing this only if absolutely necessary.
      While it does not cause clipping, please consider adding your tracking pixel at the top of your emails to ensure that opens are registered if the email clipping occurs.
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/best_practices/email_styling/#email-size
item.summary: Why do my emails get clipped/truncated in Gmail and show a 'View Entire Message' option?

comments.comment:
Hi Zach Speer - A quick clarification. The query that is included in the KA, is that something customers can do by themselves via the query builder?  (it looks like an internal snowflake table, hence I am asking) 

select * from USERS_MESSAGES_SMS_INBOUNDRECEIVE
where app_group_id = 'app-group-id'
and subscription_group_api_id = 'subscription_group_api_id'
and action = 'Unsubscribed'
and (campaign_id is not null or canvas_id is not null);

Hi Manjula Karunakaran Happy to clarify!!

The Segmenter is referring to the Braze segments dashboard for customers, and the Subscription group Timeseries is a dashboard view Section for Subscription groups.

 

My understanding was that the above query can be used by customers in Currents/ Query builder, but with adding _SHARED to the end of the table (USERS_MESSAGES_SMS_INBOUNDRECEIVE_SHARED)

item.title: [BD-4243] Low Email Open Rates
item.description:
1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding SSL click tracking setup to include additional troubleshooting tips related to SSL certificate expiration and its impact on email open rates.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation provides basic troubleshooting steps for SSL click tracking but does not address the potential issue of expired SSL certificates and their effect on email open rates.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    Email Open Rates:
          • Issue: Email open rates have suddenly plummeted.
          • Cause: The SSL certificate may have expired. Check to see if the SSL certificate has expired here: https://www.sslshopper.com/ssl-checker.html#hostname=.
          • Resolution: The SSL certificate will need to be renewed.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information regarding SSL certificate expiration and its impact on email open rates, we can help users more effectively troubleshoot issues related to SSL click tracking, thereby improving user satisfaction and reducing support inquiries.
  1. Explanations for actions (optional):
  2. Flag sensitive information:
  3. Related PQ: n/a
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/email_setup/ssl/#what-is-a-cdn-and-why-do-i-need-it
item.summary: Low Email Open Rates

item.title: [BD-4245] Clicks Logged for an Email Without an Open Event
item.description:
1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding email tracking to include information about possible reasons for delivery and clicks without an open event.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not adequately address the scenarios in which a user may show delivery and clicks without an open event, nor does it explain the implications of email client behavior on tracking.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation "Email Help Articles" to include :
      • Possible Reasons for Delivery and Clicks Without an Open Event:
        • It is possible to log a click event in an email without logging an open event. This occurs when the open tracking pixel was not loaded.
        • Explanation: Opens are tracked by hiding a unique 1px by 1px image within the email HTML. By default, this image is placed at the bottom of the email to prevent any unintentional visual style changes. An open event is logged whenever a request is made to our servers to load this pixel image.
        • A click event without an open is possible in instances when the open tracking pixel did not load. This can occur if the email is ‘clipped’ and part of the email (including the open tracking pixel) was not loaded. Additionally, if the end user has disabled images in their email client, this would prevent the open tracking pixel from loading.
        • Important Note: Some email clients do not load images by default or attempt to prevent user tracking through the industry-standard tracking pixel. For example, Apple Mail can proxy the image, causing the open tracking pixel to be loaded by the mail server first and then sent to the user upon request. The open event would be logged when the server first accessed the image, rather than when the user opens the message. Corporate email domains often prevent image loading by default due to higher security concerns.
      • Click and Open Event Timing:
        • In some cases, a click event may occur on one day while an open event is logged on a subsequent day. For example, a user may click an email on May 16th, but the open event was triggered on May 17th.
        • Explanation: Depending on the user's mail client settings, they might not log open events if they have disabled it. If users open their email multiple times from different mail clients, discrepancies may arise. For instance:
          • A user opens an email using Outlook desktop and configures the app to not download images: No open event will be logged.
          • The user then clicks on a link: A click event will be recorded, but still no open event.
          • The next day, they log into their web mail and read the same email: This action generates an open event, but from a later day.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information regarding email tracking, we can help users understand the nuances of click and open event logging, thereby improving their ability to analyze email campaign performance and reducing confusion about tracking discrepancies.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/help/help_articles/email
item.summary: Clicks Logged for an Email Without an Open Event

item.title: [BD-4247] Setting Custom Attribute as "" (blank) vs. null
item.description:
Description:
This article explains the difference between setting a Custom Attribute as an empty string ("") versus null in Braze. It highlights the implications of each choice based on the data type of the attribute and provides examples to clarify the correct usage.

Migration request:
We would like to migrate this content to Braze Docs to ensure users understand the distinction between using "" (blank) and null when setting Custom Attributes, particularly for Boolean data types. This is crucial for preventing errors in user data management.

Knowledge gap:
Currently, Braze Docs do not clearly articulate the consequences of using "" versus null for Custom Attributes, especially for Boolean types. This information is important as it can prevent users from encountering issues where attributes do not behave as expected, leading to confusion and potential data integrity problems.

Recommended actions:

  • Add a section that clearly explains the difference between setting Custom Attributes as "" and null, with a focus on Boolean data types.
  • Include examples demonstrating the correct usage of null for Boolean attributes and the implications of using an empty string.
  • Clarify that attributes set to "" will appear in user profiles as empty values, while those set to null will not be listed at all.

Flag sensitive information:
There is no sensitive information in this article that needs to be flagged for internal use only.

Related PQ:
n/a

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
Consider integrating this content into the following Braze Docs page: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/custom_data/custom_attributes
item.summary: Setting Custom Attribute as "" (blank) vs. null

item.title: [BD-4248] What is the Difference Between Estimated Audience and Current Audience?
item.description:
Description:
This article discusses the difference between "Estimated Audience" and "Current Audience" metrics in Braze, focusing on how the size of the App Group affects which metric is displayed.

Migration request:
We would like to migrate this content to Braze Docs to clarify the distinction between Estimated Audience and Current Audience metrics, particularly in relation to the size of the App Group. This will help users understand when to expect each metric and the implications of App Group size on audience measurement.

Knowledge gap:
Currently, Braze Docs do not provide a clear explanation of how the size of the App Group influences the display of audience metrics. This information is important for users to accurately interpret their audience data and make informed decisions based on the metrics presented.

Recommended actions:

  • Add a section that explains the criteria for displaying Estimated Audience versus Current Audience metrics, emphasizing the role of App Group size.
  • Include examples or scenarios illustrating when users would see Estimated Audience versus Current Audience metrics based on different App Group sizes.
  • Clarify that Estimated Audience is used when the App Group is too large for an exact count, while Current Audience is displayed for smaller App Groups.

Flag sensitive information:
There is no sensitive information in this article that needs to be flagged for internal use only.

Related PQ:
n/a.

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
Consider integrating this content into the following Braze Docs page: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/campaigns/faq/#what-is-the-difference-between-action-based-and-api-triggered-campaigns
item.summary: What is the Difference Between Estimated Audience and Current Audience?

item.title: [BD-4250] Missing UTM Parameters (Link Templates)
item.description:
Migration request: Add a troubleshooting guide for Link Templates

Knowledge gap: Our docs contain some information about Link Templates that explain the feature, but we should improve our docs by organizing it in an easy-to-access way for when problems arise. Support often gets questions about 'missing' Link Templates where the cause is a misuse or misunderstanding of the feature. There can be legitimate bugs too, but the customer and Support need to rule out user-error as quickly as possible, and a Troubleshooting guide can help facilitate that.

Recommended actions: Create a new article within Message Building by Channel -> Email -> Link Templates called "Testing and Troubleshooting Link Templates". This section should use content from the article "Missing UTM Parameters (Link Templates)" to answer the following questions:

  • How should email designers and marketers test that their Link Templates apply correctly?
  • What should they check if they notice that Link Templates are missing after they test? What could cause this, and what should they do to fix?

Flag sensitive information: I do not think this article should be copy/pasted to our public docs. This article content should be used as a guiding resource when creating a section for Link Template Troubleshooting. Some callouts:

  • The article uses examples like https://www.google.com/vegan, but the Braze public docs should use something like  https://www.braze.com/vegan instead
  • This article has 5 different missing Link Template issues, 5 different causes, and 5 different solutions. This should be formatted differently in the public docs, maybe following the Push docs as a guiding structure: https://braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platforms/android/push_notifications/troubleshooting/#troubleshooting-scenarios
  • One of the article examples uses a Support employees name in the link, I would advise that we avoid using any names, or if we have to, we could instead use generic "JaneDoe" type names. We shouldn't include Support employee information in our public docs without their consent. Example from the article: https://www.google.com/?lid=kd46j7bbdsog&query=&utm_campaign=SabinaUTM%20Test%20userid 

Related PQ: N/A

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): This should be in the Templates section. The new troubleshooting guide can link out to our existing materials on the topic:

  • Link templates
  • Link aliasing
  • Link templates FAQ
item.summary: Missing UTM Parameters (Link Templates)

item.title: [BD-4254] Invalid Email Address Causing User To Not Receive Email Campaigns
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add a link to the /email/bounce/remove docs from our docs that discuss hard bounces.
  2. Knowledge gap: We explain to customers that users' emails can hard bounce, and the information on how to remove hard bounces is available and searchable in docs, but there's currently no link between the two. If we linked out to the REST API endpoint docs from the information on hard bounces, we could cut down on customer tickets asking how to deal with email addresses marked as invalid.
  3. Recommended actions: Add something along these lines to the Hard Bounce docs (link below):
    As a general rule, hard bounces and spam complaints aren't usually false positives, and suppressing them is a critical component in healthy deliverability. Thus, when an email hard bounces or is marked as spam, that email address will be marked as invalid within Braze.

Hard bounces for marketing emails will also likely hard bounce for transactional emails because typically hard bounces are invalid email addresses. The only workaround for this is to manually remove the users from the hard bounced list using the REST API: https://www.braze.com/docs/api/endpoints/email/post_remove_hard_bounces#remove-hard-bounces.

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/report_metrics?redirected=1#hard-bounce
item.summary: Invalid Email Address Causing User To Not Receive Email Campaigns

item.title: [BD-4269] Most Recently Used Device Feature For Push Messaging
item.description:
Migration request: Add clarity for how the "Most Recently Used Device" feature works

Knowledge gap: The public docs mention this feature and how it works. But, the Knowledge Article has an additional point that is missing from our public docs - which device will Braze target? Is "most recently used device" defined by sessions, or the timestamp when the push token was imported?

Recommended actions: Update the public docs to include this information from the Knowledge Article:

  • "Enabling this feature will target push delivery for only one device, based on which device has the most recently updated Push token. It does not target the device with the most recently recorded session. For example, if a new device with a push token is added to a user via API, the new device is considered the user's "most recently used device". This is true even if the user has not logged a session on that new device before."

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): On the "Creating a Push Message" page, we should add this in the "Device options" section https://braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/push/creating_a_push_message#device-options
item.summary: Most Recently Used Device Feature For Push Messaging

item.title: [BD-4275] Optimising email HTML for Outlook
item.description:
Images are rendered in Outlook with white lines and other issues as

color: Color value is invalid

Outlook often uses Microsoft Word rendering rather than standard browser rendering, which can cause Images to render incorrectly in Outlook.

 
HTML Can be optimised for Outlook below are some resources
Remarkety: how to get rid of lines in Outlook 
Email On Acid: how to get rid of lines in Outlook 

You can also try this specific code which may help to hide the 1px line, 
!-[if (!mso) (!IE)] !- Thing you want to hide in MSO !-- ![endif]--
mso = Microsoft Office

This could be added to the FAQ page for email
item.summary: Optimising email HTML for Outlook

CSV file size Up to 100 MB for all CSV files combined across your company Up to 2 GB for a single CSV file
Characters limit for item value Up to 5,000 characters in one value. For example, if you had a field labeled description, the maximum number of characters within the field is 5,000. Up to 5,000 characters in one value. For example, if you had a field labeled description, the maximum number of characters within the field is 5,000.
Characters limit for item column name Up to 250 characters Up to 250 characters
Selections Up to 30 selections per catalog Up to 30 selections per catalog

Note: Another known limitation in Catalogs is the use of Catalog Liquid tags recursively inside catalogs. You cannot call a Catalog item that then calls a second catalog and catalog item.

item.title: [BD-4281] How to find an anonymous user in the Web SDK
item.description:
Migration request: Provide customer ways to find an anonymous user in the Web SDK.

Knowledge gap: Our docs do not mention how to find anonymous user in the Web SDK.

Recommended actions: Add a section that show customers how to find an anonymous user in the Web SDK. 

In a web browser go to In a web browser's developer tools. For example, in Google Chrome, go to View > Developer > Developer tools or use short-cut Command + Option + J on a Mac or Ctrl + Shift + I or press F12 on Windows.

Enter the below into the Console tab in the browser's Developer Tools (switching appboy for braze for SDK versions below 4.0)

braze.getDeviceId(function(id){console.log(id)})

Or you can:

  1. Open console. 
  2. Click on “Network”
  3. Type in “data/“ in the search field.
  4. Select data/ under Name to find device Id

Flag sensitive information: In the screenshots of the KA, there is no sensitive information.{}

Related PQ: N/A{}

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): Maybe add an FAQs section here  ** https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/home
item.summary: How to find an anonymous user in the Web SDK

  3. Recommended actions: Add something to the FAQ to this effect:
    My email templates appear to be missing; where did they go?
    First, make sure you're looking in the correct section of the templates page. You need to select whether you're looking at HTML templates or Drag and Drop templates. Additionally, double-check that you are in the correct workspace. A final thing to check (with the help of a Braze admin at your company) is whether your permissions have changed in a way that would block you from viewing templates.

item.title: [BD-4292] Why does my email look different in editing view than previewing, and test sending?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Provide clients with some top ways that an email built in the drag-and-drop editor may be misconfigured and causing display issues when previewing/testing the email.
  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs only have a troubleshooting section for HTML created emails and during the Testing phase. We need to expand troubleshooting for both of our email editors, and including multiple phases of the build process like previewing vs testing. 
  3. Recommended actions: Include the provided top reasons why an email may look different when building vs previewing/testing. This includes but is not limited to;
    1. HTML blocks are used and tags are not properly closed.
    2. An attribute is added to an image but does not include a 'value'.
    3. When making use of background images, ensure that these are not feathered around the edges.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): When building emails through Braze's Drag-and-drop editor, there are a lot styling and customization options. When used incorrectly, this can cause the client to see an email display differently when creating, previewing, or testing the email content. They often then need an additional pair of eyes to determine what went wrong and how to fix the content display.
  5. Flag sensitive information: the example screenshots in the article are likely from a customer's account and should not be used. New images that more clearly display the behavior should be created and used.
  6. Related PQ: n/a
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/help/help_articles/email/html_looks_strange/#extension-conflicts
item.summary: Why does my email look different in editing view than previewing, and test sending?

item.title: [BD-4296] Email Rendering or Clipping Issues (Inline CSS Check)
item.description:
1. Migration request: Warn users that the inline CSS feature may actually cause email rendering issues in some cases.
  2. Knowledge gap: I believe the existing doc should provide more explicit examples and reasons why the Inline CSS may have positive/adverse affects on the rendering of an HTML email. Currently, the doc only states that CSS Inlining can help email rendering. 
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. Provide an example of how CSS inlining can cause unintended display issues. In other words, provide clients with some clear examples of when you may want to disable this feature on an HTML email. 
      1. Here is an example from a very recent client case (see image attached). They were expecting the headshot image to remain small in size but in Preview, they are seeing the image height match the row's content height. This was unintended and fixed when disabling inline css on this email template (client's campaign example).
      2. Other examples of how inline can cause problems are listed as: "emails getting clipped in inboxes, generally not rendering properly, that elements are distorted, or are not appearing at all".
    2. Provide clients with information on what happens when you disable css inlining, so they can make an informed decision for potentially turning this off. 
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): Clients frequently ask Support for help determining why their email isn't rendering in the way they expect it to. Our public docs don't have many examples for troubleshooting these display problems. This knowledge article was created because we found scenarios were CSS Inlining being enabled caused more harm than good.
  5. Flag sensitive information: 
  6. Related PQ: 
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):  https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/html_editor/css_inline/#using-css-inlining
item.summary: Email Rendering or Clipping Issues (Inline CSS Check)

item.title: [BD-4298] Testing Content Cards in an iOS App
item.description:
1. Migration request: Provide extra details around why testing a Content Card requires the test user to be enabled/opted in to Push Notifications. 
  2. Knowledge gap: Clients are commonly confused why they need to be push enabled to test a content card. 
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. The 'Test Checklist' should have a bullet point for 'ensure your test user is opted into push with a valid push token'.
    2. 'Debug' should include a screenshot of the system warning you the test content card can't be sent because they user isn't enabled for push (see image attached). Clarify again, and link to the above 'test' callout, that your test user must be enabled for push to receive the card.
    3. Include the detail that Test content cards typically expire after ~5 minutes (assuming this is still valid based on Product confirmation).
    4. Potentially, provide extra technical context for why this Push notification is needed:
      1. "To view a test Content Card (sent using the "Test" feature in the Braze dashboard), we package the card within a Push payload, and when the SDK opens the push, it finds the card in the payload and adds it to the locally cached cards.

The reason behind this is that a "Test" send avoids the normal live send system where we add the card to the database that shows which cards are available for which users."

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): 
  2. Flag sensitive information: 
  3. Related PQ: 
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):  https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/content_cards/testing/#test
item.summary: Testing Content Cards in an iOS App

item.title: [BD-4299] Multiple IAMs Display In The Same Session if They Are Both Triggered by 'Session Start'?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Clarify the end-user experience of triggering multiple IAMs from one event, and how Braze uses priority and recency to determine the order of the IAMs to be displayed to that user.
  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs don't mention that multiple IAMs won't display for the user in the same session. We state that we display based on priority, but we should clarify that this requires the user to log a new session to get the next IAM in the priority list.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. Answer this question: Can two or more IAMs display in the same session if they both trigger off of 'Session Start'? Which is "no", one IAM per session.
    2. Further explain how priority and recency of the IAM being created will play into the order of the IAMs being displayed for the user.
      1. In other words, take the example about 3 medium priority IAMs and add that to the article. 
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): Our clients like to be very explicit in understanding what the final user experience is going to be. In this case, we haven't made it super clear that the multiple IAMs a user triggers/is elgible for, won't display in the same session. Instead, the user will need to log future sessions in order to receive the next priority IAM.
  5. Flag sensitive information: 
  6. Related PQ: 
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): "choose a priority" section of https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/in-app_messages/traditional/create/#step-7-build-the-remainder-of-your-campaign-or-canvas
    1. Alternatively, consider creating a separate doc that has examples for expected display behavior when a client has multiple IAM campaigns with the same trigger (ie session start).
item.summary: Multiple IAMs Display In The Same Session if They Are Both Triggered by 'Session Start'?

item.title: [BD-4300] Customizing Emails for Dark Mode and Light Mode
item.description:
1. Migration request: Provide the resource links from the knowledge article, as supporting resources for a client to reference when wanting to build an email that supports both light and dark themes. In addition, clarify that not all mailboxes support these stylings, like Gmail.
  2. Knowledge gap: Braze's email building product is very robust, but not all of our clients are well versed in crafting an email that meets their needs. Our docs often don't explain how to achieve certain common goals of an email. In this case, we don't have any guidance for 'how' a user should consider building their email to be compatible in a light and dark theme. We just say they can preview whatever they have already built.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. Add: Generally, emails do not need to be sent in both Light and Dark Mode styles. However, we have seen instances where emails lack explicit background colors or look broken or not as intended when the end user receiving the email is using Dark Mode themes on their device.
      1. The prefers-color-scheme CSS media query may be a place that can help with the design emails specifically for both dark and light themes. Here is a Braze Perspectives article on Dark Mode.
    2. Add: Gmail does not support the prefers-color-scheme media query. You can read the support for this query here. In addition, Litmus has a great guide on designing emails that are dark mode friendly.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): if we can provide clients with common best practices for building emails, they may rely on Braze employees less for these strategies.
  5. Flag sensitive information: 
  6. Related PQ: 
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): I think this information would benefit from being a separate article or an expansion of this 'can I preview dark mode' FAQ: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/drag_and_drop/faq#can-i-preview-how-my-email-appears-in-dark-mode
item.summary: Customizing Emails for Dark Mode and Light Mode

item.title: [BD-4301] How is Revenue Tracked in a Canvas?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Include more explicit details about how total revenue is being calculated/attributed to a canvas.
  2. Knowledge gap: Right now, the docs really only clarify how revenue is attributed to specific canvas steps/variants. It does not elaborate on how Total Revenue may not solely be based on the primary conversion set.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. Add: The Total Revenue metric in the Canvas analytics represents the sum of purchases made by users who received the particular variant within the primary conversion window.
      This means that even if a user did not perform the specified primary conversion event A but made a purchase, that purchase is still counted towards the total revenue.

As per our documentation, which you can review here, the Total Revenue is calculated based on all purchases made by Canvas recipients within the set primary conversion window, regardless of whether they performed the primary conversion event.

  1. Explanations for actions (optional): 
    1. Help clients self-serve the interpretation of revenue/conversion performance in a canvas.
  2. Related PQ: 
  3. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 
    1. Potentially build out a 'Revenue' specific section on this page: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/canvas/testing_canvases/measuring_and_testing_with_canvas_analytics/#analyze-variants{}
item.summary: How is Revenue Tracked in a Canvas?

item.title: [BD-4304] API Success Message Received But No Message Delivered
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add a bit more context to our docs about what a success message received for a REST API request means.
  2. Knowledge gap: We mention in our docs that a success message from a messaging endpoint just means the call was received for processing and why this doesn't guarantee delivery of messages. What we don't mention is that for endpoints like /users/identify, the success message just means that the call was formatted correctly, and not necessarily that the information was processed as expected.
  3. Recommended actions: Add the following context to the docs at the link below:

For example, for /users/identify, if the request synax and format is correct, it will return a success message. This means that Braze has received the request for processing. However, after processing, if there is no match for the alias, it will be dropped.

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/api/errors/#server-responses
item.summary: API Success Message Received But No Message Delivered

item.title: [BD-4307] Slowness in Displaying Action-Based Content Cards
item.description:
1. Migration request: I was kind of mixed in making this request because it may be possible this is outdated due to the introduction of the "At First Impression" feature for Content Cards. That being said we do get the occasional ticket for this and it may cut sown on those if this information is within our docs.
    2. Knowledge gap: Action Based content delivery for campaigns is not instant and its expected.
    3. Recommended actions: From the KA:
      For action-based delivery campaigns(like session start), it is expected that there will be a short but indeterminate delay between the event and the card being available. For better understanding, the backend process for content cards triggered by session start is: 
      • The campaign gets triggered by session start
      • Content card is created in database for that user
      • SDK performs a content card sync and will pull all available cards to the deviceThis means that the session start event needs to be flushed to our servers first then trigger the campaign. After the campaign gets triggered, it takes time for the content card to get created and then, there needs to be a sync to get all the available content cards. 

The alternate solution for this to mitigate the delay is to use Scheduled Delivery instead of Action-Based Delivery. However, the concern of using Scheduled Delivery is that new users are not able to receive these cards, as these users did not exist in Braze when the cards were sent. New users can only receive action triggered cards on the first session after a few seconds delay. 

In summary, the short delay is inevitable for new users in their first session, but it can be avoided for existing users with scheduled content cards. Thus, in this approach, you will have to duplicate all cards in 2 campaigns, a scheduled one for all existing users (i.e. session count > 0) and an action-triggered one to target new users (i.e. session count = 0). 
We may need to add some or even all of this information on the below page. 

    1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/content_cards/create/?tab=campaign#step-6-review-and-deploy
item.summary: Slowness in Displaying Action-Based Content Cards

item.title: [BD-4337] AM display order if trigger action and priority level are equal
item.description:
Migration request:

If 2 IAMs have the same trigger and same message priority, the one that was created more recently will be displayed to the user first. The next time the trigger action takes place the other IAM will be displayed.

 

Example:
2 IAMs both triggered on Session Start with "High" Priority. One was created on March 10th and one was created on April 9th. If the user starts a session on April 10th, the IAM from April 9th will be displayed to the user; if the user starts another session on April 15th, the IAM from March 10th will then be displayed to the user.

Explanations for actions:

Currently the docs only talk about the priority level and not what happens if the proritys are the same.

 

https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/in-app_messages/traditional/create/#step-7-build-the-remainder-of-your-campaign-or-canvas
item.summary: AM display order if trigger action and priority level are equal

item.title: [BD-4346] How Does Braze Calculate Unique Clicks for Email?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Gives the client more information around how we count as Unique clicks in email
  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs gives a brief explanation on how we calculate unique clicks. 
  3. Recommended actions: I would suggest adding more details around
    What happens after those 7 days?
  • After 7 days, we increment another number for unique click (if a click event was done for that user). We do not use any aggregate stats from the esp anymore. All events are calculated by braze now."
    If a user clicks Unsubscribe, do we track that as well?
  • Braze provided unsubscribe links are counted as 'click' events, because they are fundamentally clicking the url. Similarly, if a customer did not use the Braze unsubscribe link and used a custom link, it would also be counted. There is no 'standard' practice for this across ESP's. Some do count the unsubscribe link as a click, and some do not.
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/report_metrics#unique-clicks and https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/reporting_and_analytics/analytics_glossary#unique-clicks **
item.summary: How Does Braze Calculate Unique Clicks for Email?

item.title: [BD-4350] Identify Endpoint: User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response
item.description:
Migration request: {}Warn users of unexpected results when User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response using Braze REST endpoint  /users/identify

Knowledge gap: Our docs don’t mention unexpected results when User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response using Braze REST endpoint  /users/identify. The user alias is case-sensitive and the alias_name field must match the capitalization of the alias. If not, the request will return as 201 Success (if the call is in the correct syntax), but the alias-only user and the external ID will not be merged.

Recommended actions: Add a section provide information on unexpected results when User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response using Braze REST endpoint  /users/identify. For example:

A user with no external ID has an assigned user alias "JimJones@example.com" with the label set to "email". A request is made to our users/identify endpoint to assign an external ID to this user with the request body containing:

"alias_name:" "jimjones@example.com"
"alias_label:" "email"

Despite returning 201, the request will not assign the given external_id because the "J"s are case-sensitive. There is no user with the alias jimjones@example.com only JimJones@example.com

Flag sensitive information: There is sensitive information in the KA as one of our Support reps used their email as an example.{}

Related PQ: N/A{}

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/api/endpoints/user_data/post_user_identify/#how-it-works
item.summary: Identify Endpoint: User Alias not associated successfully with external ID after 201 response

item.title: [BD-4356] IAM on Session Start with Delay - Nuances
item.description:
The Nuances regarding IAM campaign Scheduled delays and sessions starts is not mentioned in the documentation.

Add the following :

"It is possible when an In-App Message (IAM) Campaign has a "Schedule Delay" triggered on Session Start, if the user logs a Session and then closes the app before the In-App Message is displayed, it will be triggered on the next Session Start after the delay has expired.

Something to note: This can cause potentially non-ideal In-App Message display behaviors, specifically if you have an In-App Message Campaign in which you have not selected the option to "Re-evaluate campaign eligibility before displaying".

Example:  A stopped In-App Message campaign with an 8 second delay was still being received by a user a month later. This happened because the user logged a Session and then immediately ended it, the next time they logged a Session a which was month later, the In-App Message was displayed on Session Start after 8 seconds had passed.

If a user navigates away from the app without closing it, the In-App Message will be displayed when the end use brings the app back to the foreground."

 

Braze Doc : https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/in-app_messages/about/#potential-use-cases
item.summary: IAM on Session Start with Delay - Nuances

item.title: [BD-4385] When is a user's account flagged as having uninstalled the app? When are they considered to have installed the app?
item.description:
This article explains how/ when a user uninstalls and reinstalls an app.

"Uninstall tracking "tags" users who have uninstalled the app. In the case a client uses the "has not uninstalled" filter for a campaign, these tagged users will not receive the campaign.

If a user reinstalls the app and then simply leaves the app on their device, they will still have the uninstall tag. The tag is removed once the user starts a new session."
item.summary: When is a user's account flagged as having uninstalled the app? When are they considered to have installed the app?

item.title: [BD-4386] Email text is not displaying in Dark Mode
item.description:
1. Migration request: We need to create a subsection within our Email creation docs that tackles some common issues with Dark Mode. Or add it to an FAQ section. 
  2. Knowledge gap: This is from the KA, it mainly just talks about ways around common issues in Darkmode.

I am testing an email, and on light mode everything appears as expected. However on dark mode, some text is not displayed as expected.

Example text below that doesn't fully show text


 
This can happen when email providers such as Gmail and Outlook invert colors automatically in dark mode. The email itself will often contain only the color values for the light mode rendering of the message. The email application will then attempt to convert these values according to their own rules and methods. 

Some mail apps (such as Outlook) will attempt a partial color inversion where only light-colored sections invert to darker colors. Other mail apps, (for instance, Gmail) will perform a full inversion where light colors become dark and dark colors become light.
 

Content Where a Background Image is Used

If content is displayed with a plain white background image with black text, in dark mode the email provider will invert the black text to white. However, because the color of an image cannot be inverted the image will also remain white. This will result in white text over a white background image, which will not be seen. 

To resolve this, we would recommend not including a plain background image set in white, but instead setting the background color which email providers can then invert on dark mode. 

Low Contrast Text

In rare cases, the text may appear to have insufficient contrast in dark mode. For example, the Gmail app on Android appears to convert some colors differently compared to the iOS app (Gmail for iOS shown on the left, Gmail for Android on the right).

  

In this scenario, the recommended workaround would be to adjust the colors used until a suitable combination is found.
 

  1. Recommended actions: We could probably add this to the DND editor FAQ but this may also just pertain to emails as a whole. This is something that I believe can affect HTML as well.
  2. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/drag_and_drop/faq#can-i-preview-how-my-email-appears-in-dark-mode
item.summary: Email text is not displaying in Dark Mode

This knowledge helps users manage API call volumes and ensures their systems can handle the expected load."

Recommended Actions:

"Add a section that describes the reasons for multiple Connected Content requests per message send, including validation, retry logic, and rendering requirements. Provide examples, such as the handling of plain text and HTML in emails, and explain how different channels might affect API call behavior. Include guidance on rate limiting, emphasizing the importance of setting messaging channel rate limits below the intended Connected Content rate limit to manage call volumes effectively."

Explanations for Actions (Optional):

"Clarify that Connected Content calls may be retried automatically based on specific error responses, such as 429 or 408 errors. Highlight that caching behavior can reduce duplicate calls but is not guaranteed to limit calls to one per user. Explain that the rate limit for Connected Content is tied to the message sending rate, and concurrency should be considered when multiple campaigns hit the same endpoint."

item.title: [BD-4399] Gmail Promotions Tab
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add some additional troubleshooting tips to our docs on Gmail Promotion Setup
  2. Knowledge gap: We have details in our docs on how to set up Gmail Promotions stuff, but there is additional context in this KB article that could help users self-serve when it comes to troubleshooting potential issues.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    If there are issues, there are a few things that you can check:
    • Is the script in the email editor syntactically correct? You can check using Google's Promo Tab Preview Tool.
    • Check if the Gmail Promo Tab script has been correctly passed in the raw email file. (In Gmail web, you can click on "Show Original") 
    • Are the emails landing in the Promotions tab or in the main inbox? It will only show if its in the Promotions tab
    • Are you testing on both desktop and mobile?
    • Are you following the best practices outlined here?
      Ultimately, it's Google's algorithm that determines when and who to display the card to so there is no guarantee that every recipient will see it:
    • The email could go to the promotion tab but not display the card.
    • There should be a maximum of two cards in promotions which makes it impossible to show a card for every sender that has it set up.
    • Braze doesn't get statistics for card impressions since Gmail doesn't share that data externally.
    • Gmail owns the algorithm for testing too.
    • The HTML in the email is what Braze sends to the ESP (and subsequently Google via the ESP), and we don't have control over how Google renders it from there
    • The annotations do not appear in Google Workspace Accounts
    • The annotations will only appear if the email lands in "Top Picks"
    • The annotations only appear on Mobile while using the Gmail app.
    • Recipient could be in a gmail control group (holdout group) and will not see annotations.
    • The header of the email is being stripped and the annotation gets lost (if you use the JSON script.) An easy way to check on this is by selecting “Show Original” from the tools dropdown in a recipient Gmail account and see if the email contains the desired script when you test your emails. 
    • If there are any unique parameters/query strings/ identifiers in the image url of the annotation, this might also block the annotation from rendering. Image urls may not vary across recipients for a lot of privacy and security reasons. This means that if image urls vary across recipients (by either using dynamic images or adding unique utms/ query strings) Gmail won’t display the annotations. 
    • Not all policies in DMARC are enabled. All policies in DMARC need to be enabled (quarantine/reject policies are especially important.)
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/html_editor/gmail_promotions_tab/#prerequisite
item.summary: Gmail Promotions Tab

      1. explain how to check for a specific message that may have lead to uninstalls
      2. explain how the presence of the push notification error 'BadDeviceToken' can indicate users are uninstalling the app. If they see this error in bulk around the same timeframe as the spike, this is generally a genuine report of uninstalls.

item.description:

item.title: [BD-4416] Checking the Most Recent App Version Number filter works correctly
item.description:
1. Migration request: Clarify that there are two different version numbers shown on the value for this segment filter. It is the version number you see in parenthesis for the filter that is checked, where the number outside of it is shown for convenience.
  2. Knowledge gap: Users are targeted incorrectly when using the filter Most Recent App Version Number. They have an app version not matching that set in the filter criteria. This is caused by selecting the wrong desired version, based on looking at the wrong portion of numbers in this filter. ie. you pick the value based on the number outside of the parenthesis when instead, you should pick the value shown in the parenthesis.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. Add a note and an example for which of the numbers on the filter is the one Braze uses in validating users against this filter.
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): see #2, clients who do not know this information can incorrectly target a set of users, thus needing to reach out to Support.
  5. Flag sensitive information: n/a
  6. Related PQ: n/a
  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/segments/segmentation_filters
    1. App > Most recent app version number
item.summary: Checking the Most Recent App Version Number filter works correctly

  3. Recommended actions: 
    1. Ensure we specify that if a user made an edit to the campaign or canvas, they will also receive this email (along with the Creator)
  4. Explanations for actions (optional): the docs currently don't list all eligible users who may actually receive this email.

  7. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/campaigns/building_campaigns/rate-limiting#delivery-speed-rate-limiting
    1. either update the sentence: "If the rate limit is too low, the creator of the campaign will receive alerts in the dashboard and by email."
    2. or consider creating a brand new article that describes the automated emails sent by the system and who the recipients of those notifications are.
item.summary: Who is Sent "Canvas Messages Delayed 24+ Hours" Email Notifications?

item.title: [BD-4424] Using Liquid If Statements with Catalog Items
item.description:
1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding catalog items to include information on using Liquid if statements with catalog items and selections.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not address how to effectively use Liquid if statements with catalog items and selections, which is essential for users looking to implement conditional logic in their messaging.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Using Liquid If Statements with Catalog Items:

To be able to use catalog items as a variable with liquid, you would need to first declare the catalog list before you are able to retrieve the variable to use with if statements:
Catalog Items

{% catalog_items Test-list %}
{% if items[0].first-item == true %}
Do this
{% else %}
Do that
{% endif %}
In some circumstances, the following liquid snippet may work as well:{% catalog_items Test-list %}

{% if items[0].first-item == true %}
Do this
{% else %}
Do that
{% endif %}

Catalog Selections
This example shows if custom attribute venue_name has more then 10 characters or less then 10 characters. If blank it will abort.

{% catalog_selection_items item-list selections %}

{% if items[0].venue_name.size > 10 %}
.....
{% elsif items[0].venue_name.size < 10 %}
....
{% else %}
{% abort_message(no venue_name) %}
{% endif %}
Pro tip is to use the blue + to insert catalog's liquid to prevent any mistake.

        • Explanations for Actions (optional):
          • By providing this additional information on using Liquid if statements with catalog items and selections, users will have a clearer understanding of how to implement conditional logic in their messaging, enhancing their ability to create personalized and dynamic content.
        • Flag Sensitive Information:
          • No sensitive information identified.
        • Related PQ:
          • n/a
        • Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
          • n/a
item.summary: Using Liquid If Statements with Catalog Items

item.title: [BD-4431] Dashboard error when previewing email campaigns
item.description:
1. Migration request: Warn users that browser extensions may impact their ability to preview email campaigns.
  2. Knowledge gap: Our docs don't give troubleshooting advice for the error "Sorry, you do not have permission to access this resource."
  3. Recommended actions:
    Some users may receive an error reading, "Sorry, you do not have permission to access this resource" when attempting to preview an email campaign. This is something that would be isolated to the user(s) Google Chrome browser or some extension they have is modifying or removing the
    Referer
    header. Some people do this because it is thought to enhance privacy (by hiding from which website a user was referred), but it prevents a security check against cross-site scripting attacks. To determine whether a browser extension is causing this error, try to preview the campaign in an incognito browser window. If this works, the issue is likely with an extension you have installed. Some users also find that clearing their browser cache and deleting cookies resolves this issue.
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): this might not be the best place for it, but possibly here: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/faq
item.summary: Dashboard error when previewing email campaigns

item.title: [BD-4438] Why are Multiple Connected Content Calls Occurring for a single user?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add a small detail to Connected Content FAQs
  2. Knowledge gap: We explain in our docs that we may make multiple Connected Content calls per user. We currently don't specify that only one of those calls will populate a message.
  3. Recommended actions: Add the following text to the first FAQ on the linked page: "It may be called multiple times under various circumstances, however in all cases, only one such call would actually render in the template.  Under no circumstances would multiple connected content calls be rendered in the message.  What typically happens is that a connected content call might be made, discarded for one reason or another, and then called again before rendering."
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/personalization_and_dynamic_content/connected_content/making_an_api_call/#frequently-asked-questions
item.summary: Why are Multiple Connected Content Calls Occurring for a single user?

item.title: [BD-4440] Email Open Not Tracked on Clipped Messages in Gmail
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add analytics reasoning for keeping email size under 102KB to prevent clipping
  2. Knowledge gap: Emails that get clipped can fail to register opens, if the open tracking pixel is in the part of the email that was clipped. We have guidance to keep emails under the size at which Gmail would clip them, but we don't mention that failing do to this can result in missing analytics.
  3. Recommended actions: Add some variation on this text to the email styling guidelines: 
    Gmail clips emails that have a message size larger than 102KB, and hides the full content behind a 'View Entire Message" link .

The email message is the underlying code that makes up the email. This includes your text, the full URLs and tracking code of any links, the HTML used to style your content, and more.

Each letter (or character) of the code is approximately 1 to 2 bytes. The total byte size of every character in the code creates the message size. When Gmail clips the message, it also clips the tracking code that accounts for opens.

​​​​​​​To make sure all opens are tracked, and your full campaign content isn't hidden behind a link, it is important to make your message smaller than 102KB.

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/best_practices/email_styling/#email-size
item.summary: Email Open Not Tracked on Clipped Messages in Gmail

item.title: [BD-4450] Understanding the Subscription Group Timeseries
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add some context on what the numbers in the Subscription Group Timeseries graph mean.
  2. Knowledge gap: We don't currently define these metrics very well in our docs.
  3. Recommended actions: Add the following text to the "Viewing subscription group sizes" section of the linked page below:
    • The Subscription Group Size displays the total number of users who are subscribed to a subscription group at the given date.
    • The Subscription Group Unsubscribed Size is the total amount of unsubscribed users from that specific subscription group as of that date.
      If you notice a large difference between the numbers in the Subscription Group timeseries and a segment filtering for 'Email Subscription Status is Unsubscribed', this is because a user can be subscribed to email, but not subscribed to the specific group captured by the timeseries.

This segment filter is checking the global email subscription state, while the subscription group size chart shows the number of users who are explicitly subscribed/unsubscribed to a specific group.

One important caveat is how we determine the membership count. In some cases, Braze will use the accurate count if the total number of users in this app group is under 2 million. Otherwise, the estimated count is used for the Timeseries graph.

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/managing_user_subscriptions/#archiving-subscription-groups (Viewing subscription group sizes section)
item.summary: Understanding the Subscription Group Timeseries

item.title: [BD-4460] The Difference Between "Send to Production" and "Send to Development" When Adding an Apple Push Certificate
item.description:
Migration Request:

"Explain the difference between the 'Send to Production' and 'Send to Development' options when adding an Apple Push Certificate in Braze."

Knowledge Gap:

"Currently, Braze Docs may not clearly explain the distinction between sending push notifications through Apple's development gateway versus the production gateway. This information is essential for users to correctly configure push notifications for different app environments, ensuring that notifications are delivered as intended."

Recommended Actions:

"Add a section that details the purpose of the 'Send to Production' and 'Send to Development' options when configuring Apple Push Certificates. Explain that 'Send to Development' is used for apps built in development mode, which require push notifications to be sent through Apple's development gateway. Conversely, 'Send to Production' is for apps distributed via Apple's Test Flight or AppCenter, which should use the production gateway. Include guidance on selecting the appropriate option based on the app's distribution method."

Explanations for Actions (Optional):

"Clarify that the choice between development and production gateways depends on how the app is built and distributed. Development mode apps require the development gateway, while apps distributed for testing or production should use the production gateway to ensure proper notification delivery."

Flag Sensitive Information:

"No sensitive information identified in the provided content."

Recommended Braze Docs Page (Optional):

Push FAQ: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/push/ios_push_notifications/
item.summary: The Difference Between "Send to Production" and "Send to Development" When Adding an Apple Push Certificate

Recommended Actions:

"Add a section that answers frequently asked questions about Connected Content and IAMs. Include details on when Connected Content calls are made, emphasizing that calls occur as soon as the IAM is delivered, unless delayed by checking 'Re-evaluate Campaign Eligibility before Sending'. Explain that identical Connected Content calls in different IAM buttons result in separate calls for each button. Clarify the conditions under which data added to a user profile via REST can be used in the same session as receiving an IAM, highlighting the importance of re-evaluating campaign eligibility before sending."

Explanations for Actions (Optional):

"Clarify that Connected Content calls are not tied to on-click events, even if placed in buttons, and that separate actions are required for data updates to be used in IAMs. Emphasize the need for re-evaluating campaign eligibility to ensure the most up-to-date data is used when rendering IAMs."

item.title: [BD-4463] More or Less Unsubscribes than Clicks on Unsubscribe Link
item.description:
Migration Request:

"Explain why there might be discrepancies between the number of unsubscribes and clicks on the unsubscribe link in email campaign analytics."

Knowledge Gap:

"Currently, Braze Docs may not clearly explain the reasons for discrepancies between unsubscribe counts and unsubscribe link clicks in email analytics. Understanding these differences is important for users to accurately interpret email campaign performance and user engagement."

Recommended Actions:

"Add a section that explains the potential reasons for having more or fewer unsubscribes compared to clicks on the unsubscribe link. Clarify that a higher number of unsubscribes than clicks can result from the use of list-unsubscribe headers, which count as unsubscribes without logging a click. Conversely, a higher number of clicks than unsubscribes can occur if users click the unsubscribe link multiple times, leading to repeated actions being counted."

Explanations for Actions (Optional):

"Clarify that list-unsubscribe headers provide an alternative method for users to unsubscribe, which is not reflected in click counts but is included in unsubscribe totals. Highlight that repeated clicks on the unsubscribe link by the same user can inflate click counts without affecting the total number of unique unsubscribes."

Flag Sensitive Information:

"No sensitive information identified in the provided content."

Recommended Braze Docs Page (Optional):

Email FAQ:
https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/faq/#can-i-add-a-view-this-email-in-a-browser-link-to-my-emails
item.summary: More or Less Unsubscribes than Clicks on Unsubscribe Link

See https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/platform_integration_guides/ios/push_notifications/integration for assistance generating and uploading a .p8 file. If you are confident you need to use a .p12 or . pem certificate, please reach out to our support team for assistance: https://www.braze.com/docs/support_contact.""

item.title: [BD-4482] Why are the Email 'Total Clicks' Higher Than 'Total Opens'?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add an explanation for why total clicks may be higher than total opens.
  2. Knowledge gap: Our analytics docs are unclear on this point.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    Total Opens is the count of how many times the email was opened by the users.

On the other hand, Total Clicks is the count of how many times the user clicked within the delivered email, this includes any type of clicks including link clicks.Thus, it can still be expected that Total Clicks is more than Total Opens.

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/report_metrics (under Total Clicks)
item.summary: Why are the Email 'Total Clicks' Higher Than 'Total Opens'?

item.title: [BD-4501] Why Doesn't 'Messages Sent' Number Match Campaign CSV Export?
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add a callout to our Campaign CSV Export docs about why the numbers might not line up exactly with campaign analytics.
  2. Knowledge gap: I'm not seeing anywhere in our docs where we explain the couple of instances where this could happen, would be an easy win for customer education.
  3. Recommended actions: 
    You may be concerned that the number of Messages Sent on a campaign's analytics page does not match with the CSV exported of user data from the campaign. What could be causing this mismatch?
    There are two main reasons why the unique recipients/messages sent figure will not match the user data export CSV:
    1. If re-eligibility is (or was at one point) turned on
    2. Users have been deleted (or merged) since the campaign / canvas was sent 
    1. If users are (or were at one point) able to receive the campaign more than 1 time, this is likely the reason that the Campaign Analytics numbers and the number of users included the user data export don't line up. While Messages Sent includes all messages sent for the campaign, regardless of unique recipients, the CSV output contains user profile data for every recipient of the campaign. For example, if the number of Messages Sent is 12 and there are 10 users in the CSV export, either two users received the campaign twice or one user received the campaign three times.
    2. The CSV export give us a snapshot of currently existing users who have received a given campaign. But because users can be deleted, the CSV export count will sometimes be lower than the unique recipient count. So for example, if 1000 users receive a campaign, the campaign should have 1000 unique recipients, and if we check the CSV export that same day, we should also see 1000 users. If a month later, 50 of these 1000 users get deleted, CSV export would now contain 950, whereas the incremented unique recipient count would still be 1000.
  4. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/export_braze_data/export_campaign_results_data/#campaign-recipients (campaign recipients section)
item.summary: Why Doesn't 'Messages Sent' Number Match Campaign CSV Export?

item.title: [BD-4502] Push Notification Titles Appear Correctly on Android but not iOS
item.description:
1. Migration request: Add to the push troubleshooting docs.
  2. Knowledge gap: I'm not seeing this information about formatting push titles differently for Android vs iOS in our docs currently, and this feels like an important callout.
  3. Recommended actions: 

Issue
When using Liquid to send a customised push campaign, the push displays correctly and in full for Android. However, for iOS, the title seems to be cut off as soon as any Liquid variable is reached.For example: a customised title reading "Regarding your flight from Italy to France", where Italy and France are Liquid variables, appears as expected on Android.

On iOS, this would read "Regarding your flight from".

Resolution
When Android parses a string to be inserted into a push title, it automatically cleans the title of any whitespace on the left and right-hand side, tabs, all newlines, and so on (known as stripping a string).

Conversely, on iOS, newlines (\n) are not filtered out. So, if a title reads "This is a\n test title", the title will appear to be "This is a test title" on Android but "This is a" on iOS.

Rather than trying to rebuild the Liquid without including any newlines, you can use the Liquid strip_newlines filter to remove any newlines included in the string automatically. As a result, these removed newlines will not corrupt the title and the entire title may be rendered correctly in an iOS push.

One technique to more easily achieve the desired result is to wrap it with a capture command. This makes it easier to add the strip_newlines filter.

Example:
Create the capture (named x):
​​​​​​{% capture x %} YOUR LIQUID HERE {% endcapture %}Call the capture in your message, while removing the newlines:
{{ x | strip_newlines }}

  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/push/troubleshooting
item.summary: Push Notification Titles Appear Correctly on Android but not iOS

item.title: [BD-4514] Testing Nested Custom Attributes and Nested Objects for Custom Event Properties
item.description:
Migration request: Explain how to test with Nested Custom Attributes and Nested Objects for Custom Event Properties

Knowledge gap: Our docs explain what Nested Custom Attributes are, and how to import them to Braze. But they don't explain how to test messaging that references a Nested Custom Attribute or Nested Objects for Custom Event Properties via liquid. Our Preview & Test tool doesn't support adding mock data for this. We should be in the habit of clearly outlining how customers should test features and encouraging them to do so - it's easier to fix a problem if it's caught in early testing than if it's post-launch

Recommended actions: Add a section called "Testing Nested Custom Attributes" and "Testing Nested Objects" to the relevant feature pages. This could be written step-by-step or a video walk-through

"Testing Nested Custom Attributes"

Prerequisites: campaign that references Nested Custom Attributes via liquid

  1. import the nested attributes to the user profile you're using to test
  2. on your campaign, navigate to "Preview and Test"
  3. preview the message as the user from step 1

"Testing Nested Objects"

Prerequisites: campaign that is triggered by or references Nested Objects, targeting your test users

  1. launch your test campaign to your test audience
  2. log the custom event with nested object to your test user's profile

Flag sensitive information: N/A

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):

  • Nested custom attributes-> add a section for "How to test with nested custom attributes"
  • Nested object in custom events -> add a section for "How to test with nested objects in custom events"
item.summary: Testing Nested Custom Attributes and Nested Objects for Custom Event Properties
