item.title: [BD-4534] Image Not Showing Up on iOS Push Notification
item.description:
1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding troubleshooting missing push notifications to include information about rich push notifications and image display issues.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not address the specific requirements for rich push notifications on iOS and the potential reasons images may not display in push notifications.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Rich Push Notifications:
        • For images to show in a push notification on iOS, rich push notifications must be configured. If the image is not showing, check if the rich push is configured.
        • Reference Documentation: For more information, please refer to the following link: Braze Rich Notifications Documentation.
      • Other Reasons for Missing Images:
        • Some other reasons push notification images may not be showing include:
          • The file size for the image is too big.
          • The aspect ratio is incorrect.
          • The text exceeds the maximum message length.
          • The title text exceeds the maximum title length.
        • Reference Documentation: For guidance on image specifications, please refer to the following link: Braze Image Specifications Documentation.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information regarding rich push notifications and common image display issues, users will have a clearer understanding of how to troubleshoot missing images in push notifications, thereby improving their ability to effectively engage users through this channel.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • n/a
item.summary: Image Not Showing Up on iOS Push Notification

item.title: [BD-4535] Submitting a “Request Timed Out” Support Ticket
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding troubleshooting "Request Timed Out" errors to include specific information on what to include when raising a ticket to Braze Support on the Campaign FAQ page and the Canvas FAQ page
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not provide detailed guidance on how to effectively report "Request Timed Out" errors to Braze Support, which can hinder timely resolution of issues.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Information to Include When Raising a Ticket for "Request Timed Out" Errors:
        1. Screen Recording:
          • Provide a screen recording of the steps taken before seeing the error message. This helps Braze Support reproduce the error on their end.
        2. Step-by-Step Summary:
          • Include a written summary of the exact steps taken from the very beginning before encountering the error.
        3. Timestamps:
          • Provide the exact timestamp(s) of when the error occurred, including the timezone.
        4. Link to Dashboard Page/Campaign/Canvas:
          • Include a link to the specific Dashboard page, Campaign, or Canvas where the error message appears.
        5. Frequency of the Error:
          • Outline how often the "Request Timed Out" error occurs. Is it intermittent or constant? Is it blocking any actions (e.g., launching/saving Canvases)?
        6. Browser Console Screenshot:
          • Take a screenshot of the browser console when the error message appears. To access the console, right-click anywhere within the browser window and select “Inspect.” Then, go to the “Console” tab and capture any errors or warnings that appear when the "Request Timed Out" error is displayed.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information on how to report "Request Timed Out" errors, users will be better equipped to communicate issues to Braze Support, leading to quicker resolutions and improved user experience.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • n/a
item.summary: Submitting a “Request Timed Out” Support Ticket

            • Did you previously log into the portal before?
            • If not, how long has it been since you were assigned as a user of the portal?
            • Did you follow the link in your email to set a password for the portal?
        • Resolution:

  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):

item.title: [BD-4537] Zero Sends for In-App Message Canvas
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding in-app messages in Canvas to include information on why a Canvas may show no sends occurred while impressions are logged.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not explain the discrepancy between sends and impressions for in-app messages within a Canvas, which can lead to confusion for users analyzing campaign performance.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Understanding In-App Message Sends and Impressions:
        • Validation Status: Validated
        • Issue:
          • Messages sent are always zero in the Analytics dashboard for a Canvas containing an in-app message step.
        • Cause:
          • The delivery of in-app messages is different compared to other channels such as email or push notifications.
        • Resolution:
          • In-app messages are 'pulled' by the SDK, rather than 'pushed' from the Braze platform.
          • An in-app message for an eligible user is delivered automatically on session start, ready for the trigger event. Since all eligible users will 'receive' the message if they start a session, we don't report this as a 'send' event.
          • If the user subsequently performs the trigger event, the message would be shown. We then log an impression and also mark the Campaign (or Canvas step) as received on the user profile.
          • Therefore, the send total is expected to be zero for in-app messages.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):

item.title: [BD-4538] HTML email templates with content blocks not rendering properly
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Create a troubleshooting guide for the HTML editor to address issues with HTML email templates that contain content blocks not rendering properly.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not provide guidance on troubleshooting rendering issues for HTML email templates that include content blocks, which can lead to confusion and frustration for users.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • HTML Email Templates with Content Blocks Not Rendering Properly:
        • Summary:
          • HTML email templates that contain content blocks are not rendering properly in any inbox or in preview.
        • Solution:
          • Check each content block to ensure there are no additional doctype headers.
          • If there is a doctype header in the HTML template itself along with an HTML doctype in one of the content blocks, the email will not render properly.
          • Treat content blocks as additional HTML being added to the email template, not as a brand new HTML doctype. This means the content block should not contain extra body tags or any of the template HTML code.
          • In some instances, customers may use Emailify to import 'pre-written' code blocks, which can result in extra code. Also, confirm there are no duplicated tags and class names.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this troubleshooting information regarding HTML email templates and content blocks, users will be better equipped to resolve rendering issues, leading to improved email performance and user satisfaction.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/html_editor
item.summary: HTML email templates with content blocks not rendering properly

item.title: [BD-4540] Not receiving a test email send (abort message tag)
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding aborting messages to include a warning about why a test email send may not be received due to abort message logic.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not explain the reasons why a test email may not be received when abort logic is in place, which can lead to confusion for users testing their email setups.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Warning: Not Receiving a Test Email Send (Abort Message Tag):
        • Summary:
          • This guide outlines the reason a test send will not be received by a user.
        • Validation Status: Validated
        • Warning Message:
          • If you are not receiving a specific test email and see a message on the preview feature indicating an abort, this is expected behavior. The liquid setup contains abort logic that activates if the user profile in question does not contain the required attributes/events.
        • Resolution:
          • To work around this behavior, customers can be advised to preview a user who contains the required data and produces an email outcome in the preview. Then, override this in the test section by ticking the option to use the preview user information.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information regarding test email sends and abort message logic, users will have a clearer understanding of how to effectively test their email campaigns, leading to improved campaign performance and user satisfaction.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/personalization_and_dynamic_content/liquid/aborting_messages/#query-for-abort-messages
item.summary: Not receiving a test email send (abort message tag)

item.title: [BD-4541] Catalogs use cases Vs Catalogs Selections use cases
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding Catalogs and Catalog Selections to include guidance on structuring Catalog data based on end use cases and the differences between standard Catalog data and Catalog Selections.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not provide clear guidance on how to effectively structure Catalog data for various use cases, nor does it explain the differences between using standard Catalog calls and Catalog Selections.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Structuring Your Catalog Data in Braze:
        • When planning how to structure your Catalog data in Braze, it's important to think backwards from your end use case. Each row in the Catalog refers to an “item” (with a unique ID). The columns in the Catalog should contain information relating to that “item,” such as URLs, description copy, image URLs, price, rating, size, color, etc.
      • Catalogs:
        • With standard Catalog calls, you match a value against the ID column. By dynamically inserting a Custom Attribute or Event Property (in the form of an ID string) into the Catalog call, you can pull multiple pieces of information relating to one item (or row) a user has engaged with into the message for personalization. This opens up use cases such as:
          • Recent product/service viewed ID
          • Wishlist items ID
          • Deals by location ID
          • Product purchased ID
          • Lifecycle stage ID
          • Last searched product/service ID
          • Content by days since sign up
      • Catalog Selections:
        • Another way to leverage your Catalog data is through the Selections feature. After creating your Catalog, you can apply a series of filters to the data in your other columns to run queries against the Catalog and return up to 10 items (rows) that meet the specified criteria. To make the items returned personal to a user, insert Custom Attributes or Event Properties into the filters to make this dynamic. Here, you are matching values in any of the columns in the Catalog, rather than just the ID column.
        • Depending on the data a user has logged on their profile, each user will receive a different combination of recommendations. Main use cases include:
          • Up to 10 items where category equals X
          • Brand equals X
          • Cuisine equals X
          • Size equals X
          • Subscription type equals X
          • Average order value equals X
          • Destination/location equals X
          • Any column in your table…
      • Additional Use Cases:
        • Q: Customer wants to display messages that are two days after send time. The catalog selection filters pull a time attribute, but there are no instructions on how to fill it in or no way to have it be dynamic depending on send time. Is it true that this is not possible with catalog selection either?
          • A: You can use Liquid for the value of the filter. For example, this would filter items based on today's date:

liquid
Copy
{{{{{{ 'now' | date: "%Y-%m-%d" }}}}}}

        • Use Cases NOT Supported:
          • There is a field from catalogs (e.g., Event Address) that does not populate for every single entry. If a customer wants to pull a catalog selection of every event that has an Event Address not blank, this is not supported with the catalog selection filters. There is no filter for a value not being set. Customers can submit a Product Request for this.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information on structuring Catalog data and the differences between standard Catalog calls and Catalog Selections, users will have a clearer understanding of how to effectively utilize their Catalogs in Braze, leading to improved personalization and campaign performance.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/personalization_and_dynamic_content/catalogs
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/personalization_and_dynamic_content/catalogs/selections/#use-case
item.summary: Catalogs use cases Vs Catalogs Selections use cases

item.title: [BD-4542] Troubleshooting layout breaks within the drag and drop editor
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation to include troubleshooting tips for layout breaks within the drag and drop editor for emails.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not provide guidance on troubleshooting layout issues that may arise when using the drag and drop editor, which can lead to confusion for users experiencing rendering problems.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Troubleshooting Layout Breaks Within the Drag and Drop Editor:
        • Summary:
          • This article covers some troubleshooting tips for when the layout of an email breaks in the drag and drop editor.
        • Validation Status: Validated
        • Issue:
          • My drag and drop email seems to be breaking layout for different email clients.
        • Cause:
          • The layout break could be occurring because the generated HTML/CSS from the drag and drop editor may be incompatible with the custom HTML.
        • Important to Know:
          • All emails are built via HTML/CSS.
          • The Braze drag and drop editor generates HTML/CSS behind the scenes.
          • You have the ability to add custom HTML via the HTML block.
        • Resolution:
          • Step 1: Check if the drag and drop email includes any custom HTML/CSS. This may not play well with HTML/CSS generated from the drag and drop editor.
          • Step 2: Remove all custom HTML. If the layout renders as expected within inbox vision, then the issue is with the custom HTML and must be reviewed.
          • Step 3: Check if a custom font has been loaded into basic styling within Drag-and-Drop Email Editor Settings. Specifically, open the font URL in a new browser and see if there are styles being loaded from the CSS. This could cause conflicts within preview mode but not in test or live sends.
          • Step 4: Check if columns in each row have varying padding sizes. (Note: You can check this by navigating to the row properties.)
        • Something to Note:
          • Sometimes the custom HTML may be directly placed or indirectly placed via a content block.
            • Example Direct: HTML elements directly placed within the HTML block.
            • Example Indirect: Content block referencing custom HTML outside of the drag and drop email.
        • Common Breaks Found in Custom HTML:
          • Fixed image size.
          • Images need to be fluid/responsive.
          • Width of the table exceeding the width of the drag and drop email.
          • The HTML is not using a table-based layout.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information on troubleshooting layout issues within the drag and drop editor, users will have a clearer understanding of how to resolve rendering problems, leading to improved email performance and user satisfaction.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/faq/#can-i-add-a-view-this-email-in-a-browser-link-to-my-emails
item.summary: Troubleshooting layout breaks within the drag and drop editor

item.title: [BD-4543] Email was deferred due to the following reason(s): [IPs were throttled by recipient server]
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding email delivery issues to include information on the error message related to IP throttling and how to reduce this error.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not explain the implications of receiving a deferred email error due to IP throttling by the recipient server, nor does it provide guidance on how to mitigate this issue.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Email Deferred Due to IP Throttling:
        • Summary:
          • This section explains what the error message "Email was deferred due to the following reason(s): [IPs were throttled by recipient server]" means and provides suggestions on how to reduce this error.
        • Resolution:
          • Customers may experience high soft bounces, with a majority of the bounces attributed to the aforementioned deferred email error. This could indicate that the sending rate exceeds what the recipient's server will allow based on the sender's reputation.
          • To improve the situation, it is recommended to utilize Braze's Rate-limiting feature, which can help manage the sending speed and reduce the likelihood of IP throttling.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information regarding email deferrals and IP throttling, users will have a clearer understanding of how to address delivery issues, leading to improved email campaign performance and reduced bounce rates.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/reporting_and_analytics/email_reporting/#email-performance
item.summary: Email was deferred due to the following reason(s): [IPs were throttled by recipient server]

item.title: [BD-4544] How to delete a segment of users with a webhook
item.description:
color: Color value is invalid

Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding webhooks to include detailed instructions on how to delete a group of users using a webhook, along with warnings about rate limits and other considerations.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not provide comprehensive guidance on the process of deleting users via webhooks, including necessary precautions and limitations associated with the deletion process.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Deleting a Group of Users Using a Webhook:
        • Summary:
          • This article details how to delete a group of users using a webhook and contains warnings about hitting rate limits.
        • Validation Status: Validated
        • Resolution:
          • User deletion is usually carried out by creating a webhook campaign with the segment of users to delete as its audience. The payload of the webhook would be aimed at the User Delete endpoint and would use Liquid to dynamically pull in the braze_id into the braze_ids field.
          • Ensure that the webhook delivery is rate-limited to respect API limits and avoid overloading servers.
        • Additional Considerations:
          1. Users in Segment to be Deleted:
            • Ensure the segment accurately represents the users you want to delete, as deleted users cannot be restored. Be cautious with dynamic filters that may change the audience.
            • Consider importing a specific list of users via CSV using the User Import tool for better targeting.
          2. Rate Limiting:
            • The webhook campaign must respect the rate limits shared with other endpoints. Ensure that no more than 20,000 requests are made to the group of endpoints per minute while the campaign is sending.
            • Confirm with your development team about the usual call volume to these endpoints and consider setting a limit close to 10,000 requests per minute for safety.
          3. Total Users to be Deleted:
            • Estimate the time required to delete users based on the rate limit. For example, deleting 1.5 million users at a rate of 10,000 per minute could take several hours. Consider splitting the deletion into multiple campaigns.
            • If deleting over 5 million users, contact your CSM or Support team beforehand to inform them of the mass deletion.
          4. API Key:
            • Ensure the API key has the correct permissions to be used for the webhook deletion.
        • Step-by-Step Instructions:
          1. Create a webhook campaign targeting the segment of users to delete.
          2. Enter the appropriate REST endpoint for your cluster in the Webhook URL field (e.g., {{{} {REST ENDPOINT}

            /users/delete{}}}).

          3. Under "Request Headers," enter:
            • Content-Type => application/json
            • Authorization => Bearer [APIKEY]
          4. In the Raw text field, enter:

json
Copy
{{{"braze_ids" : ["{{$

{braze_id}

}}"]}}}

          1. Thoroughly test the campaign with a small subset of users to ensure it functions as expected, keeping in mind that test users will also be deleted.
  2. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information on deleting users via webhooks, users will have a clearer understanding of the process and necessary precautions, leading to more effective user management and reduced risk of errors.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/webhooks
item.summary: How to delete a segment of users with a webhook

item.title: [BD-4546] Are Link Templating and Link Aliasing supported in Drag n Drop Editor ?
item.description:
Description: This is more of a formatting/consistency suggestion. The content from the KA is present in the docs. 

Migration request: The Link Template docs have a section titled "Using link templates in email campaigns" and a set of instructions for HTML Editor and Drag-and-Drop Editor. I don't feel that these instructions are that different from each other, so this section of the docs can likely be tightened up. 
After your link templates are set up, you can select the template to use in your email.

HTML editor: Go to the Link Management tab under the Content section. Select Add a Link Template, choose your link template, and select Add.
important:
To access the Link Management tab in the updated HTML email editor, you must have link aliasing turned on. To turn on link aliasing, contact your account manager.
Drag-and-drop editor: Select Content > Link Management tab. Then, select Add a Link Template. To access link templates in the drag-and-drop editor, you must have link aliasing turned on.

After your link templates are set up, you can select the template to use in your email.

  • HTML editor: Go to the Link Management tab under the Content section. Select Add a Link Template, choose your link template, and select Add.

 
 

Recommended actions: To make it more concise, we could:

  • Edit the purple "Important" text box to say: To access link templates in the updated HTML email editor or the Drag-and-Drop editor, you must have link aliasing turned on. To turn on link aliasing, contact your account manager
  • Combine the two bullet points into one: Create an email campaign using the Drag-and-Drop Editor or HTML Editor. Under the Content section, select the Link Management tab.  Select Add a Link Template, choose your link template, and select Add.
  • Consider adding a video walk through or gif
item.summary: Are Link Templating and Link Aliasing supported in Drag n Drop Editor ?

item.title: [BD-4564] Background image in Drag & Drop (DND) email editor not rendering in outlook windows.
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding Inbox Vision to include information about background images in the Drag & Drop (DND) email editor not rendering in Outlook for Windows by adding this as a note/tip on this page: https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/drag_and_drop/overview/#step-4-test-your-email
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not address the compatibility issues with background images in the DND email editor, specifically for Outlook Windows users.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Background Image in Drag & Drop Email Editor Not Rendering in Outlook Windows:
      • {}Certain email clients tested by BeeFree (our DND vendor) do not support background images, with Outlook Windows users being one of them.
        • Solution:
          • Email Client Compatibility:
            • The BEE editor uses advanced email design techniques to ensure that background images render on most email clients. According to recent tests, the only email clients where background images were not shown include:
              • Outlook desktop on Windows.
              • Background images work on Outlook 2011 & Outlook 2016 on OS X, and on outlook.com.
              • Lotus Notes 7.
              • free.fr email client.
              • T-Online.de email client.
              • Xfinity / Comcast email client.
            • For those email clients, ensure that you set a background color for the row that works well in the absence of the background image.
        • Reference:
          • BeeFree Background Image Documentation
        • Environment:
          • Email, DND editor.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information regarding background image rendering issues in the DND email editor, users will have a clearer understanding of email client compatibility, leading to improved email design and user experience.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.

item.title: [BD-4565] Email For Segment Export Of User Data Not Arriving In Inbox
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding export troubleshooting to include information on issues related to receiving segment exports from the dashboard.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not address specific troubleshooting steps for users experiencing issues with receiving segment exports from the Braze dashboard.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Issue: Problems Receiving Segment Exports from the Dashboard:
        • Cause:
          • This can be due to a number of issues.
        • Resolution:
          • When troubleshooting these issues, consider the following:
            1. Ensure you can export the segment data and that the email arrives in your inbox.
            2. If you don't receive the export, this may indicate an issue on Braze's end. Depending on the export size, the timing for delivery may vary. If the email hasn't arrived after an hour or so, reach out to Braze's Support team.
            3. Check if the issue is widespread on your end. Is there anyone on your team who can receive the export?
            4. Check your spam folder.
            5. The emails should come from no-reply@alerts.braze.com. Add that address to your safe sender list to see if that resolves the issue.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information on troubleshooting segment export issues, users will have a clearer understanding of how to resolve problems related to receiving exports, leading to improved user experience and efficiency.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/export_braze_data/export_troubleshooting/#common-errors

item.title: [BD-4567] How to Set Up Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) When It Is Not Enforced or Optional
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation to include instructions on how to set up Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for users when it is not enforced or is optional, along with images for clarity. (Not sure if this is the most latest guide but the main idea is to update to the most current way of setting up 2fa with instructions and visuals)
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not provide clear guidance on how users can manually activate Two-Factor Authentication when it is not enforced by the administrator.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Setting Up Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) When It Is Not Enforced:
        • Instructions:
          1. Download the Authy App for Android, iOS, or desktop.
          2. Log into the Braze dashboard.
          3. Click your name in the top right of the screen to open the drop-down menu.
          4. Click Account Settings.
          5. Scroll to or find Two-Factor Authentication and click Start Setup.
          6. You'll be redirected to a login modal. Enter your password and click Check Password.
          7. You'll be redirected to a Two-Factor Authentication Set Up modal. Enter your phone number and click Enable.
          8. The modal will prompt you to enter the 7-digit code from Authy. Enter the code into Braze's Two-Factor Authentication Set Up modal and click Verify.
          9. Optional: If you enable Remember this account for 30 days, you will not be prompted to enter 2FA again for 30 days.
          10. Setup Complete.
        • Note: There may be a new process that I'm not aware of, but the main idea is to update on how to set up Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) when it is not enforced or optional, with images and instructions for easy understanding.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information on setting up Two-Factor Authentication, users will have a clearer understanding of how to enhance their account security, leading to improved overall security practices within the Braze platform.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • n/a
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/administrative/app_settings/company_settings/security_settings/#company-settings

item.title: [BD-4584] Limitations for IAM DnD Editor
item.description:
Knowledge Request

  1. Migration Request:
    • Enhance the Braze documentation regarding in-app messages (IAM) to include a section on the limitations of the Drag and Drop (DnD) editor for IAM.
  1. Knowledge Gap:
    • The current documentation does not provide a comprehensive list of limitations specific to the IAM DnD editor, which can lead to confusion for users trying to utilize this feature effectively.
  1. Recommended Actions:
    • Update the documentation to include:
      • Limitations for IAM DnD Editor:
        • Summary:
          • A list of limitations for the IAM DnD Editor.
        • Resolution:
          1. Adding Deep Links:
            • Currently, there is no way to add different deep links for different devices in the DnD IAM editor (unlike the traditional editor).
          2. Adjusting Opacity for Background Images:
            • The Opacity feature on the Drag and Drop IAM builder does not allow for complete transparency of background images (as the traditional IAM editor does). However, the message background color with opacity controls is available.
          3. DnD IAM Max Width:
            • The minimum max width in the DnD is 325px, primarily meant as the limit for the preview in the dashboard. It should display properly on smaller screen devices < 325px.
          4. Different Backgrounds for Different Platforms:
            • It is not possible to show two different backgrounds for the same message on different platforms (e.g., web and mobile).
          5. Message Styles:
            • Background images apply to the full message and cannot be customized per page. “Message styles” apply to the entire message, not individual pages.
          6. Measurement Unit for IAM Height for Spacer:
            • The measurement unit is Pixel (px). There is no option to change the unit for spacer.
          7. Only Modal and Fullscreen IAM Supported:
            • Currently, only Modal and Fullscreen IAM formats are supported. Please submit product feedback if you wish to include other formats.
          8. Background Image Stretches the IAM:
            • The modal will adjust to fit the size/aspect ratio of the background image. Change the size/aspect ratio as needed.
          9. Background Image is Persistent Across Pages:
            • This is expected behavior. Customers wanting multi-page IAMs with their own creative assets as a full image on each page must add a button to allow users to click to the next page.
        • Internal Notes:
          • Slack Links and Notes for Reference
          • Spacing gap between text in IAM: Users can choose px or em unit to fix spacing gaps, as the % unit for ‘Letter Spacing’ was removed due to CSS validity.
          • IAM DnD Editor - Text rendering into multiple lines is intended behavior due to auto-resizing on different devices. Adjust font size to prevent text from breaking into multiple lines.
  1. Explanations for Actions (optional):
    • By providing this additional information on the limitations of the IAM DnD editor, users will have a clearer understanding of the capabilities and restrictions, leading to more effective use of in-app messaging features.
  1. Flag Sensitive Information:
    • No sensitive information identified.
  1. Related PQ:
    • https://servicedesk.braze.com/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/12/PQ-8217
  1. Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/in-app_messages/best_practices/prep_guide/#general-considerations
item.summary: Limitations for IAM DnD Editor

item.title: [BD-4621] What FCM Responses are treated as uninstall push?
item.description:
Migration request: Include examples of FCM requests that would count as an 'uninstall' in Braze

Knowledge gap: Our docs say that Braze tracks uninstalls by observing when push messages sent to users’ devices return a 'signal' from either Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) that the app is no longer installed. But what does that signal look like? What are some examples of the responses from FCM that we'd consider an uninstall?

Recommended actions: Add some examples of FCM responses that Braze considers an uninstall. We treat the following responses from FCM as token removal responses:

  • DEVICE_UNREGISTERED
  • BAD_REGISTRATION
  • SENDER_ID_MISMATCH

We should add the same for APNS, though this knowledge article only discusses Android.

Flag sensitive information: I feel we should omit the part where we discuss "dry_run == true"

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 

  • https://braze.com/docs/user_guide/analytics/tracking/uninstall_tracking/#uninstall-tracking-for-campaigns
  • https://braze.com/docs/developer_guide/push_notifications/troubleshooting/?sdktab=android
item.summary: What FCM Responses are treated as uninstall push?

  • Deeplinks from push clicks opening the app's home page instead of the desired destination, when the app is backgrounded

item.title: [BD-4625] If a Campaign is Failing Inbox Vision, What Should be Done to Fix It?
item.description:
Migration request: Clarify how customers should think about their Inbox Vision spam test results, and what they should do if they see a failed spam test in Inbox Vision

Knowledge gap: Our docs just say how to run a spam test in Inbox Vision. The Article states how the tests work (i.e. sending the message to a seed account), and clarifies that the results should be viewed with a grain of salt

Recommended actions: Add a section that informs customers what to do if they see a failed Inbox Vision spam test, and how to think about these results.

Under Inbox Vision -> Spam Testing, add content to clarifying how these spam tests are run and how the customer should think about these results. Something like:

  • These spam tests rely on static seed accounts to verify whether the mail appears or not. By nature, seed accounts don't engage with mail by opening or clicking. Note that recipient engagement is the primary driver of inbox placement according to the major inbox providers. Therefore, the results of these tests may not always align with the inbox placement that you observe from live campaigns or canvases. 
  • A more reliable approach to understanding email deliverability is to conduct multivariate content testing with small cohorts of live users. High open and click rates are the most accurate indicator of whether your emails are reaching the inbox.
  • While spam tests can provide some insights, Braze recommends monitoring recipient engagement and conducting live testing for a more comprehensive understanding.
  • link to other sections of docs on Managing Deliverability / improving inbox placement

Flag sensitive information: We should take caution with how we phrase this. The goal is to help the customer to understand how the feature works and how to think about the test results, not to discount it's reliability. We should omit phrasing from the article like: "This type of spam test by its nature is not terribly reliable. In the past, we've compared them to personality quizzes in Buzzfeed and Facebook"

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):

  • https://braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email/inbox_vision/#spam-testing
item.summary: If a Campaign is Failing Inbox Vision, What Should be Done to Fix It?

item.title: [BD-4628] What Are the Reasons for Delayed Push Notifications on Android and iOS?
item.description:
Migration request: List some potential causes for a delayed push notification

Knowledge gap:  There are a few different causes for a delayed push notification. Our docs outline the push sending pipeline but don’t mention what a customer should check if they perceive a delay.

Recommended actions: Add a section to the Push Troubleshooting page for Swift and Android that outlines some potential causes for a delayed push notification. The article lists the following reasons:

  • Mobile device has weak data connection
  • The app has custom code which can suppress Braze push notification
  • User device settings or preferences for push notifications
  • Message priority of the push when created in the campaign or canvas
  • Traffic delays or issues with the Push Service Providers (FCM and APNS)

To identify and resolve push delay issues, try breaking things down the push pipeline broadly in terms of steps:

  1. Braze processes that the end user should be sent the notification. This could be delayed because of processing the trigger event or delayed messaging traffic.
  2. Braze sends this push payload to the Push Service Provider (APNS or FCM). The provider could be experiencing high levels of traffic; that information is obfuscated to Braze.
  3. The provider sends the push to the device. Delays could occur from a weak data connection, local device settings, or push customization. Verbose logs can narrow this down.

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): https://braze.com/docs/developer_guide/push_notifications/troubleshooting **
item.summary: What Are the Reasons for Delayed Push Notifications on Android and iOS?

item.title: [BD-4629] Engagement Report Not Sending
item.description:
Migration request: Add a section to the Engagement Reports doc that specifies that filters will need to match at least one campaign or canvas in order to run the report. They report will error out if there is no match, because there's no data to export.

Knowledge gap: Our docs don’t mention that the Engagement Report settings need to match at least one campaign or canvas in order to properly run if they're using immediate send. Customers would see an error and may not realize why. It feels like it should be rather intuitive, but worth calling out if customers are opening tickets about this.

Recommended actions: In the Engagement Reports page under the "Step 2 Add Messages" -> "Automatically select campaigns or Canvases" section, ** add a green "Note" box that says:

  • The filters will need to match at least one campaign or canvas in order to run the report. If you've selected "Send Immediately" and are seeing an error, double check the tags selected. Make sure that at least one campaign or canvas matches your chosen criteria.

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 

  • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/analytics/reporting/engagement_reports/#step-2-add-messages
item.summary: Engagement Report Not Sending

Recommended actions: Add a section to our Email docs that defines BIMI, and directs customers to the resources needed to set this up. We should mention that Braze is not part of this setup directly and is unable to provide technical support for this implementation. Resources:

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):

item.description:
• Migration request:

  • Knowledge gap: 
    • Some customer reach out to ask support to turn-off link alias completely. However, they do not know the impact of turning this feature of
  • Recommended actions:
    1. Add a section that outlines the following if Braze turns off link aliasing
      1. All new messages or content blocks that are created won’t have markup anymore
      2. All existing messages that were created since the feature will still have their markup, though you’ll have to manually remove these
      3. If you have link templates, you’ll have to re-add them if you update an existing message/campaign
      4. Click reporting may look wonky/inaccurate for messages that were launched while the feature was on
      5. Segments that use link aliasing filters may break
  • Flag sensitive information: Just within the internal notes

    1. Add an additional FAQ to the docs outlining that making changes to max recipient to an active Campaign may cause misalignment with send analytics . If the maximum recipient limit was added post-launch, the campaign may have been respecting the rule at the time of the send. If the recipient limit is changed after the messages are enqueued, the original rate limit would be applied. If the sends are also rate limited, this could lead to some unexpected behavior.

    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/engagement_tools/segments/creating_a_segment/#considerations
item.summary: Nested Array Custom Attribute Segmentation Filters Not Working Correctly

item.title: [BD-4653] How Do I Retrieve the Users Who Have Entered and re-entered a Canvas/Canvas Step?
item.description:
• Migration request: 
    • Provide users with additional options for exporting users that have re-entered a canvas and the number of times a user re-entered a canvas
  • Knowledge gap: 
    • The current canvas export docs does not provide options on how to export users that have re-entered a canvas (use canvas details) when re-eligibility is enabled and how to understand the amount of times a user a entered (Use Currents)
  • Recommended actions: 
    • Add a notes banner with following 
      • If re-eligibility is enabled and you would like to have the first and last time the user entered, they can use the API endpoint [ /canvas/details|https://www.braze.com/docs/api/endpoints/export/canvas/get_canvas_details/#prerequisites]
      • If you would like to know all the times the user entered the canvas, you should use Currents. There is an event called Entry - Canvas entry events. If you do not have Currents integrated, please, reach out to your CSM Team.
  • Explanations for actions (optional): 
    • we've had questions in the past where users want to export list of user that entered a canvas step 
  • Flag sensitive information: N/A
  • Related PQ: N/A
  • Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/user_guide/data/export_braze_data/export_canvas_data/#exporting-data-for-a-canvas
item.summary: How Do I Retrieve the Users Who Have Entered and re-entered a Canvas/Canvas Step?

item.title: [BD-4657] Is there any way to filter out "false clicks"?
item.description:
Migration request: Clarify what "false clicks" or "bot clicks" are, and how to spot them

Knowledge gap: Our public docs have a page titled "Handling increases in click rates", but this page doesn't clearly outline the telltale indicators of security software activity. We should enable marketers to be able to identify this on their own and have a documented resource for next steps. There is a great blog post on this topic, but the blog posts don't show up in docs searches. I think we should centralize the info from the blog post into our public docs since this is such a common issue.

Recommended actions:  

  • Add a section to the "Handling increases in click rates" page that defines "non-human interaction" or "bot clicks":
    • An email "bot click" occurs when a click on a hyperlink within an email is generated by an automated program, or security software. This is commonly referred to as "bot" or "web crawler" activity rather than an action taken by a human recipient.
  • Outline telltale signs of security software activity. If a marketers sees an inflated click rate, they should review their analytics with the below telltale signs of security-software activity in mind: 
    • Time to Click -> If an email is opened or clicked in less than a second from when it was delivered, it is likely that a machine rather than a human is behind that activity
    • Total links clicked -> Security software or bots tend to click every link within an email. If your data shows that a single user clicked every link in an email within a short timespan, is likely that a machine rather than a human is behind that activity
    • Recipient type -> Security software activity is more common when sending to domains with an increased need for security, such as those belonging to businesses, government agencies, and educational institutions

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional):

  • https://www.braze.com/docs/help/help_articles/email/open_rates#handling-increases-in-click-rates 
  • Blog post with helpful information: https://www.braze.com/resources/articles/bot-or-not-understanding-email-bot-clicks
item.summary: Is there any way to filter out "false clicks"?

item.title: [BD-4665] How do Senders Resolve a Block at Microsoft?
item.description:
Migration request: Provide a guide for customers to follow to help them navigate a block from Microsoft

Knowledge gap: Email marketers can experience blocks from inbox providers like Microsoft, especially if they aren't following deliverability best practices. Our docs mention the best practices they should be following, but they don't explicitly outline what a customer should do if they find themselves blocked. This creates a burden on Support and Deliverability teams to help them navigate the situation

Recommended actions: 

  • Similar to ticket https://braze.atlassian.net/browse/BD-4226, we should expand the Email Section of our docs to address Deliverability issues
    **
  • Add a section under Message Building by Channel -> "Email" -> called "Resolving Blocks by Inbox Providers" that includes the information in this Knowledge Article "How do Senders Resolve a Block at Microsoft?"

Recommended Braze Docs page (optional): 

  • https://braze.com/docs/user_guide/message_building_by_channel/email
item.summary: How do Senders Resolve a Block at Microsoft?

comments.comment: This topic is very similar to the content in this other Knowledge Article [How can I fix bad email sender reputation?|https://braze.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VP000000LPWTYA4/view with 46 associated cases. I recommend that we use the content from both articles in the new "Resolving Blocks by Inbox Providers" page.
